Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: MFP planned revenue and margin are tracked via profit centers by channel/brand. Financial reporting requires linking merchandise financial plans to profit centers for segment P&L planning and performa

Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Merchandise financial plans are seasonal planning entities; season_code and season_year exist but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant attribut
Name or employee identifier of the merchandising leader or finance officer who formally approved this merchandise financial plan.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Workday HCM

description: The date on which the merchandise financial plan was formally approved by the merchandising leadership and finance team. Required for audit trail and OTB budget release.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; IFRS/GAAP

description: Percentage of total planned inventory allocated to this specific distribution channel (DTC retail, DTC e-commerce, wholesale, outlet). Drives channel-level receipt planning and allocation strategies in Oracle Retail.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System Allocation; Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module

description: Timestamp when this merchandise financial plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary plan values are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency planning for international wholesale and DTC channels.
Fiscal quarter or period identifier within the fiscal year to which this plan applies (e.g., FY2025-Q1). Supports quarterly OTB budget reviews and financial reporting.
The company's fiscal year to which this merchandise financial plan belongs. May differ from calendar year depending on the company's fiscal calendar (e.g., fiscal year ending January 31).

Indicates whether this merchandise financial plan has been locked and frozen for execution. When True, no further edits are permitted and the plan serves as the official baseline for OTB budget management and performance tracking.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this merchandise financial plan within Anaplan and Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Used for cross-system reconciliation and business reference.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The last date of the planning period covered by this merchandise financial plan. Marks the end of the season's selling window and the EOM (End of Month) inventory target date.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the merchandise financial plan, typically including season, year, department, and channel (e.g., 'FW2025 Womens Apparel DTC Retail Plan').
The first date of the planning period covered by this merchandise financial plan. Marks the beginning of the season's selling window for OTB and inventory planning purposes.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Current lifecycle status of the merchandise financial plan. Draft indicates work-in-progress; submitted indicates pending approval; approved indicates finance/merchant sign-off; locked indicates frozen for execution; archived indicates superseded.

Indicates whether the plan was generated using a top-down (executive-driven targets cascaded down), bottom-up (buyer/planner-driven builds rolled up), or reconciled (merged top-down and bottom-up) planning approach.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Version identifier of the merchandise financial plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Supports iterative planning cycles where plans are revised across pre-season, in-season, and post-season phases.

Planned AUR (Average Unit Retail) representing the expected average selling price per unit across the department/class/channel combination. Drives unit sales plan derivation from the net sales dollar plan.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management

business_glossary_term: Planned Beginning of Period (BOP) Inventory Amount
description: Planned inventory value at retail at the beginning of the planning period. Used as the opening stock position for OTB calculations and inventory flow modeling.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

business_glossary_term: Planned Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Amount
description: Planned COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) for the season, representing the total cost of merchandise expected to be sold. Used to derive planned gross margin and GMROI targets.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; IFRS/GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO

business_glossary_term: Planned End of Period (EOP) Inventory Amount
description: Planned inventory value at retail at the end of the planning period (EOM). Represents the target closing stock position and feeds into the next season's BOP. Critical for OTB and WOS (Weeks of Supply) planning.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

business_glossary_term: Planned Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI)
description: Planned GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) representing the gross margin dollars generated per dollar of average inventory investment. Core KPI for evaluating merchandise productivity and capital efficiency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Planned gross margin in absolute dollar terms (planned net sales minus planned COGS). Represents the financial contribution of the merchandise category before operating expenses.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; IFRS/GAAP

description: Planned gross margin expressed as a percentage of planned net sales (planned gross margin / planned net sales). Key KPI for merchandising performance and pricing strategy evaluation.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; IFRS/GAAP

description: Planned IMU (Initial Markup) percentage representing the difference between the cost of merchandise and its original retail price, expressed as a percentage of retail. Establishes the pricing floor before markdowns.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management

description: Planned number of times inventory is expected to sell through and be replenished during the season (COGS / average inventory at cost). Drives OTB budget and receipt flow planning.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Total planned markdown (MD) dollars for the season representing the anticipated reduction from original retail price to drive sell-through. Directly impacts MMU and gross margin performance.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management

description: Planned markdown as a percentage of planned net sales. Used to benchmark markdown aggressiveness across departments and channels and to set MMU expectations.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management

description: Planned MMU (Maintained Markup) percentage representing the actual markup achieved after markdowns and promotions, expressed as a percentage of net sales. Reflects the realized pricing performance versus IMU.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management

business_glossary_term: Planned Average Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP)
description: Planned average MSRP (Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price) across the SKUs in this department/class/channel plan. Establishes the retail price anchor for IMU calculation and consumer-facing pricing.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management; FTC Textile and Apparel Labeling Requirements

description: Total planned net sales revenue (gross sales minus returns and allowances) for the season, department, class, and channel combination. Core financial target in the merchandise financial plan. Expressed in the plan's currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; IFRS/GAAP; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Planned OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget in dollars representing the amount of merchandise the buyer is authorized to purchase for the season. Calculated as planned EOP inventory + planned sales + planned markdowns - planned BOP inventory.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Total planned merchandise receipts (inbound inventory) at retail value for the season. Represents the planned flow of new inventory into the channel to support the sales plan.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Planned STR (Sell-Through Rate) expressed as a percentage of units sold versus units received. Target sell-through rate used to calibrate buy quantities, markdown timing, and end-of-season inventory positions.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Total planned number of units expected to be sold during the season for the department/class/channel combination. Derived from planned net sales divided by planned AUR. Feeds into allocation and replenishment planning.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Planned WOS (Weeks of Supply) representing the number of weeks the current inventory position is expected to last at the planned rate of sale. Used to identify over/under-stock risk and trigger replenishment actions.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Indicates the planning phase this plan represents: pre-season (initial buy plan before season starts), in-season (revised plan during active selling), or post-season (actuals reconciliation and hindsight).
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Planned pricing strategy for this channel and merchandise category: full_price (MSRP-driven), promotional (planned promotional events), value (everyday low price positioning), or outlet (clearance/off-price). Informs IMU and MMU targets.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management; Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module

description: Timestamp when this merchandise financial plan record was last modified. Tracks iterative plan revisions across pre-season, in-season, and post-season planning cycles.
reference: GDPR Article 5; Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning Module

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the merchandise hierarchy. Primary key for the merchandise_hierarchy data product in the Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORM) silver layer.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Merchandise hierarchy nodes (department, class, subclass) map to GL accounts for automated COGS, inventory, and revenue account determination. This is a fundamental apparel finance operational link us

Foreign key linking to logistics.hs_code. Business justification: Merchandise classification for import duty: merchandise_hierarchy.hs_code_prefix is a denormalized plain attribute. Proper FK to logistics.hs_code enables category-level duty rate defaulting, CPSC reg

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent node in the merchandise hierarchy. A Division node has a null parent; a Department references its Division; a Class references its Department; a Subclass references its Class. Enables hierarchical roll-up for OTB, GMROI, and financial planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Indicates which sales channels this merchandise hierarchy node applies to: all channels, retail stores, e-commerce (DTC digital), wholesale, or direct-to-consumer (DTC). Governs channel-specific assortment planning, OTB allocation, and pricing strategies in Oracle Retail and Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the Class level node within a Department (e.g., 'JKT' for Jackets, 'PNT' for Pants). Null for Division and Department-level records. Used in Oracle Retail for class-level assortment planning, price management, and STR analysis.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Descriptive name of the Class (e.g., 'Jackets', 'Pants', 'T-Shirts', 'Sneakers'). Null for Division and Department-level records. Displayed in Oracle Retail assortment planning, allocation, and price management modules.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

business_glossary_term: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) Regulated Flag
description: Indicates whether products in this merchandise hierarchy node are subject to Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) safety standards (e.g., childrens apparel flammability, drawstring regulations). Triggers mandatory safety testing and compliance documentation requirements in Infor PLM and quality management workflows.
reference: Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) — Product Safety Standards for Apparel; CPSC 16 CFR Part 1610

description: Timestamp when this merchandise hierarchy record was first created in the Oracle Retail Merchandising System and ingested into the Databricks silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and change management tracking.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; ISO 8601; IFRS/GAAP Audit Trail Requirements

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the Department level node (e.g., 'ACT' for Activewear, 'OTW' for Outerwear). Null for Division-level records. Used in Oracle Retail for department-level OTB budgeting, allocation strategies, and merchandise financial planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Descriptive name of the Department (e.g., 'Activewear', 'Outerwear', 'Denim', 'Footwear'). Null for Division-level records. Denormalized for flat reporting in assortment planning, OTB dashboards, and Anaplan merchandise financial plans.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the top-level Division to which this hierarchy node belongs (e.g., 'MNS' for Mens, 'WMN' for Womens, 'KDS' for Kids). Populated at all levels for denormalized reporting and OTB budget allocation in Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Descriptive name of the top-level Division (e.g., 'Mens', 'Womens', 'Kids', 'Accessories'). Denormalized at all hierarchy levels to support flat reporting in Oracle Retail, Anaplan, and BI dashboards without requiring joins.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: The date after which this merchandise hierarchy node is no longer active for new planning, OTB, or SKU assignment. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active node. Supports hierarchy versioning and historical reporting in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: The date from which this merchandise hierarchy node becomes effective and available for use in assortment planning, OTB budgeting, and SKU assignment in Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Supports temporal validity management for hierarchy restructuring.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Corresponding material group or product category code in SAP S/4HANA (MM module) that maps to this merchandise hierarchy node. Ensures alignment between Oracle Retail merchandise taxonomy and SAP procurement, inventory valuation, and COGS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Group; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Indicates whether products in this merchandise hierarchy node require fiber content disclosure on product labels per FTC Textile and Apparel Labeling regulations. Drives compliance requirements in Infor PLM tech pack specifications and product labeling workflows.
reference: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) — Textile Fiber Products Identification Act; FTC Textile and Apparel Labeling Requirements

description: Gender classification associated with this merchandise hierarchy node, used to segment assortments, OTB budgets, and marketing campaigns. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System division and department structures for gender-based merchandise financial planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy; Apparel Industry Classification Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies this hierarchy node within the Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Used as the business key in OTB budgets, assortment plans, and financial reporting across Oracle Retail and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Numeric depth of this node in the hierarchy tree: 1=Division, 2=Department, 3=Class, 4=Subclass. Supports hierarchical traversal, roll-up aggregation, and level-specific filtering in OTB and GMROI reporting.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Indicates the level of this node within the four-tier merchandise classification structure: Division > Department > Class > Subclass. Governs how OTB budgets, assortment plans, and GMROI reporting are structured and rolled up in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy; National Retail Federation (NRF) Merchandise Hierarchy Standards

description: Human-readable name of the merchandise hierarchy node (e.g., 'Mens Activewear', 'Outerwear', 'Footwear'). Displayed in assortment planning, OTB reporting, and financial planning dashboards in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Current lifecycle status of this merchandise hierarchy node. Active nodes are available for assortment planning, OTB budgeting, and SKU assignment. Inactive or archived nodes are retained for historical reporting but excluded from new planning cycles in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

business_glossary_term: Never Out of Stock (NOS) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether products in this merchandise hierarchy node are designated as Never Out of Stock (NOS) items requiring continuous replenishment. NOS-eligible nodes receive dedicated OTB budget allocation and automated replenishment triggers in Oracle Retail and Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Allocation; NOS Planning Standards

description: Indicates whether this merchandise hierarchy node is designated for private label (own-brand) products as opposed to national or licensed brands. Affects sourcing strategy (CMT, OEM, ODM), IMU targets, and margin planning in Infor PLM and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Infor PLM — Sourcing

description: Labor compliance certification standard required for suppliers producing goods in this merchandise hierarchy node (e.g., FLA, WRAP, SA8000, SEDEX). Governs supplier qualification, factory audit requirements, and sourcing decisions in SAP S/4HANA MM and Infor PLM sourcing modules.
reference: Fair Labor Association (FLA) — Labor Standards Compliance; Worldwide Responsible Accredited Production (WRAP); ISO 45001

description: Timestamp when this merchandise hierarchy record was last updated in the Oracle Retail Merchandising System or the Databricks silver layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; ISO 8601; IFRS/GAAP Audit Trail Requirements

description: Lifestyle or end-use category classification for this hierarchy node, distinguishing athletic performance, lifestyle, luxury, workwear, casual, and outdoor segments. Supports brand positioning, collection planning, and targeted marketing strategies in Adobe Experience Platform and Anaplan.
reference: Apparel Industry Classification Standards; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Markdown (MD) strategy designation for this merchandise hierarchy node, indicating the planned approach to price reductions during the selling season. Governs Oracle Retail Price Management markdown rules, Anaplan financial planning assumptions, and MMU maintenance targets.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management; Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning

description: Free-text business description of this merchandise hierarchy node, providing additional context about the product category, its scope, and any special planning or sourcing considerations. Maintained in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and used in buyer-facing assortment planning documentation.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Broad merchandise type classification indicating the nature of products within this hierarchy node. Drives category-specific planning rules, margin targets, and allocation strategies in Oracle Retail and Anaplan merchandise financial planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy; NRF Merchandise Classification Standards

description: Indicates the Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget planning methodology applied to this merchandise hierarchy node: top-down (executive-driven), bottom-up (buyer-driven), or hybrid. Determines how Anaplan merchandise financial plans are structured and reconciled with Oracle Retail OTB budgets.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — OTB

description: Corresponding category code in the Infor PLM or Centric PLM system that maps to this merchandise hierarchy node. Enables cross-system alignment between the Oracle Retail merchandise taxonomy and the PLM product design and BOM management classification for collection planning and tech pack organization.
reference: Infor PLM — BOM Management; Centric PLM — Collection Management

description: Seasonal classification indicating which selling seasons this hierarchy node is active for. Drives seasonal buy plan creation, OTB budget phasing, and collection planning cycles in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Numeric ordering sequence for displaying this hierarchy node among its siblings within the same parent. Controls the display order in Oracle Retail Merchandising System screens, assortment planning tools, and reporting dashboards.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this merchandise hierarchy record originated (e.g., ORM for Oracle Retail Merchandising System, SAP for SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage, master data management reconciliation, and silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks lakehouse.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP S/4HANA; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the Subclass level node within a Class (e.g., 'PUFF' for Puffer Jackets, 'WNDBRKR' for Windbreakers). Null for Division, Department, and Class-level records. The most granular level for SKU assignment, OTB allocation, and STR tracking.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

description: Descriptive name of the Subclass (e.g., 'Puffer Jackets', 'Windbreakers', 'Slim Fit Jeans'). Null for Division, Department, and Class-level records. Used in Oracle Retail for the most granular assortment planning, allocation, and markdown optimization.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Merchandise Hierarchy

business_glossary_term: Target Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI)
description: Target Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) for this merchandise hierarchy node, calculated as gross margin dollars divided by average inventory cost. A primary KPI for merchandise financial planning and OTB budget justification in Anaplan and Oracle Retail.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System; IFRS/GAAP

description: Target Initial Markup (IMU) percentage for products within this merchandise hierarchy node, representing the planned markup from cost to MSRP at the time of first receipt. A key financial planning metric in Anaplan OTB and Oracle Retail Price Management for setting pricing strategy and GMROI targets.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Price Management; IFRS/GAAP

description: Target Sell-Through Rate (STR) percentage for this merchandise hierarchy node, representing the planned proportion of received inventory sold within a season. Used in Oracle Retail and Anaplan to evaluate assortment performance, trigger markdown decisions, and set OTB buy quantities.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Target Weeks of Supply (WOS) for this merchandise hierarchy node, representing the planned number of weeks of inventory on hand relative to forecasted sales rate. Drives replenishment triggers, OTB phasing, and allocation strategies in Oracle Retail and Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Allocation; Anaplan Demand Planning

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the otb_budget data product.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Foreign key linking to store.store_cluster. Business justification: OTB budgets in apparel are built at store cluster level for cluster-wide open-to-buy management (e.g., outlet cluster OTB vs. full-price cluster OTB). Cluster-level OTB planning is a named merchandisi

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Multi-entity apparel companies scope OTB budgets to a legal entity (company code) for statutory financial reporting and intercompany inventory transfer planning. Finance requires company code on OTB b

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: OTB budgets require cost center assignment for departmental budget control and overhead allocation. Finance uses cost center on OTB budgets to track planned receipt costs by department and reconcile a

Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: DC-level OTB receipt planning for wholesale and DTC channels: OTB budgets are planned by DC to manage inventory flow into specific fulfillment nodes. Apparel planners build DC-level OTB to align plann

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: OTB budgets drive planned receipt accruals posted to specific GL accounts. Finance reconciles OTB actual receipts against GL for inventory receipt accruals and period-close. Apparel finance teams requ

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: OTB budgets are maintained within a specific accounting ledger for period-close reconciliation of planned vs. actual receipts. Multi-GAAP apparel companies require ledger assignment on OTB budgets to

Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_financial_plan. Business justification: OTB budgets are the tactical monthly execution of the strategic merchandise financial plan. The MFP sets top-down financial targets (planned sales, COGS, GM%, IMU%, markdown budget) for a season/depar

Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: OTB budgets have department_id, class_id but no FK to merchandise_hierarchy master. Adding merchandise_hierarchy_id FK normalizes merchandise classification and removes redundant hierarchy attributes.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: OTB budgets drive planned receipts that directly impact divisional P&L. Finance requires profit center assignment on OTB budgets to reconcile planned inventory investment against divisional gross marg
Reference to the channel or location (e.g., retail store, e-commerce, wholesale) for which this OTB budget is defined. Enables channel-level OTB tracking across DTC and wholesale.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Reference to the merchandise season (e.g., Spring/Summer 2025, Fall/Winter 2025) to which this OTB budget belongs. Drives seasonal buy plan alignment in Anaplan.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The actual merchandise receipt value at cost consumed by confirmed purchase orders (POs) for the OTB budget month to date. Used for open-to-buy cost-basis tracking and cash flow management. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP S/4HANA MM; IFRS/GAAP

description: The actual merchandise receipt value at retail price consumed by confirmed buy plans and purchase orders (POs) for the OTB budget month to date. Compared against planned receipts to compute remaining OTB. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP S/4HANA MM; Anaplan

description: The variance threshold percentage (e.g., 0.10 = 10%) at which a merchant alert is triggered for this OTB budget record. When actual receipts or sales deviate from plan by more than this threshold, an alert is raised for merchant review and OTB recalculation.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Indicates whether a merchant alert has been triggered for this OTB budget record due to variance exceeding the defined alert threshold. True = alert active and pending merchant review; False = within acceptable variance range.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The unique identifier of the Anaplan model and workspace from which this OTB budget record was sourced. Enables traceability back to the originating Anaplan planning model for reconciliation and audit.
The name or employee identifier of the merchant or planning manager who approved this OTB budget record. Required for financial control governance and audit trail.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; FLA — Labor Standards Compliance; IFRS/GAAP

description: The date on which this OTB budget record was formally approved by the merchant or planning manager. Marks the transition from submitted to approved status and authorizes buying activity.
The value of on-hand inventory at cost (landed duty paid) at the beginning of the OTB budget month. Used for COGS tracking and gross margin analysis. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS/GAAP

description: The value of on-hand inventory at retail (selling) price at the beginning of the OTB budget month. Used as the opening inventory position in the OTB formula: OTB = Planned EOM Inventory + Planned Sales + Planned Markdowns - BOM Inventory. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management; IFRS/GAAP

description: The calendar month (1–12) to which this OTB budget record applies. OTB is recalculated monthly; this field identifies the specific planning month within the budget year.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Current lifecycle state of the OTB budget record. Draft: under construction by planner. Submitted: pending merchant approval. Approved: authorized for buying. Locked: frozen for the period. Closed: period ended. Revised: re-opened for adjustment.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Version number of the OTB budget record, incremented each time the budget is revised or re-submitted. Supports version control and audit trail for merchandise financial planning changes in Anaplan.
The fiscal or calendar year (e.g., 2025) to which this OTB budget record applies. Used for annual merchandise financial planning and year-over-year variance analysis.

The distribution channel for which this OTB budget is defined. Retail: physical stores. E-Commerce: digital storefront. Wholesale: B2B partners. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer): brand-owned channels. Outlet: off-price stores.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: The date and time when this OTB budget record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and compliance with IFRS/GAAP record-keeping requirements.
reference: IFRS/GAAP; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity and accountability)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this OTB budget record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent currency denomination across retail and cost values.
The last date through which this OTB budget record is effective. Typically the last day of the budget month. After this date, the budget is closed and actuals are reconciled.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The first date from which this OTB budget record is effective and binding for merchandise purchasing decisions. Typically the first day of the budget month.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The planned ending inventory value at retail price at the end of the OTB budget month. This is the target stock position that feeds into the next month's BOM inventory and is a key driver of the OTB formula. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The Initial Markup (IMU) percentage applied to convert retail values to cost values for this OTB budget period. IMU = (Retail - Cost) / Retail. Used to derive OTB at cost from OTB at retail and to assess margin health.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management

description: The exact date and time when the OTB budget values were last recalculated in Anaplan. Supports audit trail and ensures merchants are working with the most current OTB position.

Free-text notes entered by the merchant or planner to document assumptions, exceptions, or rationale for OTB budget decisions (e.g., promotional event impact, supply chain disruption, vendor delay). Supports audit trail and planning transparency.

The remaining open-to-buy dollars at cost available for the OTB budget month. Derived from OTB at retail adjusted by the Initial Markup (IMU) percentage. Used for purchase order (PO) commitment and cash flow management. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; SAP S/4HANA MM; IFRS/GAAP

description: The remaining open-to-buy dollars at retail price available for the OTB budget month. Calculated as: Planned EOM Inventory + Planned Sales + Planned Markdowns - BOM Inventory - Actual Receipts at Retail. A positive value indicates buying capacity; negative indicates over-bought. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System; IFRS/GAAP

business_glossary_term: Planned Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI)
description: The planned Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) for the OTB budget period. GMROI = Gross Margin / Average Inventory at Cost. Measures the return generated per dollar of inventory investment. Used to prioritize department and class-level OTB allocations.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The planned markdown (MD) dollar amount at retail for the OTB budget month. Markdowns reduce the retail inventory value and are a key input to the OTB formula and Maintained Markup (MMU) calculation. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management; IFRS/GAAP

description: The total planned merchandise receipt value at cost (landed duty paid / LDP) for the OTB budget month. Used for cash flow planning, purchase order (PO) commitment tracking, and COGS forecasting. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; SAP S/4HANA MM; IFRS/GAAP

description: The total planned merchandise receipt value at retail price for the OTB budget month. Represents the maximum retail value of goods the merchant plans to receive. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The planned net sales revenue at retail price for the OTB budget month, as defined in the merchandise financial plan. Drives the OTB calculation and sell-through rate (STR) targets. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System; IFRS/GAAP

description: The planned number of units to be sold during the OTB budget month. Used alongside planned sales at retail to derive the Average Unit Retail (AUR) and validate unit-level buy quantities.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning

description: The planned Sell-Through Rate (STR) for the OTB budget month, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.75 = 75%). STR = Units Sold / (Beginning Inventory Units + Received Units). Used to validate receipt plans and assess inventory productivity.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning

description: The planned Weeks of Supply (WOS) at the end of the OTB budget month. WOS = EOM Inventory Units / Average Weekly Sales Units. Used to assess inventory coverage and prevent stockouts or overstock situations.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Allocation

description: The date on which the weekly OTB recalculation was last triggered for this budget record. OTB is recalculated weekly in Anaplan based on updated actual receipts, sales actuals, and revised forecasts.

The operational system of record from which this OTB budget record originated. Anaplan is the primary planning system; Oracle Retail provides actuals integration; SAP S/4HANA provides cost and PO data; Manual indicates planner-entered overrides.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP S/4HANA

description: The date and time when this OTB budget record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Tracks the most recent update for change management, audit trail, and downstream pipeline refresh detection.
reference: IFRS/GAAP; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity and accountability)

description: The variance between planned receipts at retail and actual receipts at retail for the OTB budget month. Positive = under-received vs plan; Negative = over-received vs plan. Triggers merchant alerts when variance exceeds the defined threshold. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The variance between planned sales at retail and actual sales at retail for the OTB budget month. Used to assess sales performance against plan and trigger OTB recalculation when variance exceeds the alert threshold. Expressed in the operating currency.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP Customer Activity Repository

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the merchandise season record. Primary key for the season master reference table in the merchandising domain.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Master

description: The distribution channel scope for which this season is planned. 'All' covers all channels; 'DTC' covers direct-to-consumer retail and e-commerce; 'Wholesale' covers wholesale and distribution partners. Drives channel-specific OTB allocation and assortment planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: The date by which all clearance selling for this season must be completed. After this date, remaining inventory is subject to return-to-vendor (RTV), off-price channel transfer, or write-off. Defines the final boundary of the season's sell-through window.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: The creative or commercial theme name assigned to this season's collection (e.g., 'Urban Explorer', 'Coastal Luxe'). Defined during collection planning in Centric PLM and used to align product design, marketing campaigns, and visual merchandising narratives.
The timestamp when this season record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary planning amounts for this season are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and Anaplan.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO

description: The official last trading date of the merchandise season — the date on which the season's assortment is retired from active selling. Defines the close of the OTB budget window and triggers end-of-season markdown cadence and clearance planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Calendar

description: The earliest scheduled date for merchandise receipts into the distribution center or stores for this season. Defines the opening of the delivery window and is a key milestone in the Time and Action (T&A) calendar for sourcing and procurement coordination.
reference: Centric PLM — T&A Calendar; Manhattan Associates WMS — Inbound Planning

description: The fiscal half-year period (H1 or H2) to which this season is aligned. Spring/Summer and Resort seasons typically fall in H1; Fall/Winter, Pre-Fall, and Holiday seasons in H2. Used for semi-annual merchandise financial planning (MFP) and OTB budget allocation in Anaplan.
reference: Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The fiscal year to which this season belongs for financial planning and reporting purposes (e.g., 2025). Aligns season-level OTB budgets and merchandise financial plans (MFP) to the corporate fiscal calendar in Anaplan and SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — Fiscal Year; IFRS/GAAP Reporting Standards

description: The primary gender target classification for this season's collection, used to segment assortment planning, allocation strategies, and marketing campaigns. Aligns with product hierarchy classifications in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Centric PLM.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning; Centric PLM — Collection Management

description: Indicates whether this season is currently active and available for use in planning, buying, and allocation transactions. Set to True when the season status is 'active'; False for planning, closed, or archived seasons. Used to filter active seasons in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Master

business_glossary_term: Is Never Out of Stock (NOS) Season Flag
description: Indicates whether this season includes a Never Out of Stock (NOS) or carry-over program, where core styles are replenished continuously regardless of seasonal boundaries. NOS seasons require separate OTB budget allocation and replenishment planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning

description: The latest permissible date for merchandise receipts for this season. Defines the close of the delivery window. Receipts after this date may be subject to late delivery penalties or cancellation per vendor agreements. Key milestone in the T&A calendar.
reference: Centric PLM — T&A Calendar; SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order Management

description: The planned frequency of markdown (MD) events during the clearance phase of this season. Drives the markdown calendar in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management and informs maintained markup (MMU) forecasting in Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management; Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning

description: The date on which the first planned markdown (MD) event for this season begins. Initiates the end-of-season clearance cadence. Drives markdown (MD) planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management and Anaplan merchandise financial planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management; Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning

description: The end date of the industry market week associated with this season. Together with market_week_start_date, defines the window during which wholesale buyers finalize orders and assortment commitments.
reference: Apparel industry market week calendar standards; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The start date of the industry market week (trade show / buying week) associated with this season, during which buyers review collections and place wholesale orders. Key date for collection planning and wholesale distribution calendars.
reference: Apparel industry market week calendar standards; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The version identifier of the approved merchandise financial plan (MFP) associated with this season in Anaplan (e.g., 'v1.0-Initial', 'v2.1-Revised'). Tracks plan revisions through the planning cycle and ensures alignment between OTB budgets and financial targets.

business_glossary_term: Number of Planned Stock Keeping Units (SKUs)
description: The total number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) planned for this season across all styles, colors, and sizes. Used to manage assortment breadth, warehouse capacity planning in Manhattan Associates WMS, and OTB unit buy planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: The total number of distinct styles (SKU parent-level products) planned for this season's assortment. Set during collection planning in Centric PLM and used to manage line architecture, OTB depth-versus-breadth trade-offs, and assortment productivity analysis.
reference: Centric PLM — Collection Management; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning

description: The final date by which all purchase orders (POs) for this season must be submitted to suppliers to ensure on-time delivery within the season's delivery window. Critical T&A calendar milestone managed in SAP S/4HANA MM and Centric PLM.
reference: Centric PLM — T&A Calendar; SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order Management

description: The total approved Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget in the company's reporting currency for this season. Represents the maximum planned merchandise spend authorized for buying activities. Set during merchandise financial planning (MFP) in Anaplan and tracked against actual PO commitments in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.
reference: Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — OTB

description: The planned gross margin percentage for this season as set in the merchandise financial plan (MFP). Calculated as (planned sales minus planned cost of goods sold) divided by planned sales. Drives GMROI targets and IMU/MMU planning in Anaplan.

The planned initial markup (IMU) percentage for this season, representing the difference between the cost of goods and the initial retail price (MSRP) as a percentage of retail. Set during merchandise financial planning and used to guide pricing strategy in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management; Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning

description: The total planned net sales revenue in the company's reporting currency for this season as set during merchandise financial planning (MFP). Serves as the top-line revenue target against which actual sell-through rate (STR) and average unit retail (AUR) performance are measured.
reference: Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning; IFRS/GAAP Revenue Recognition Standards

description: The planned sell-through rate (STR) percentage target for this season, representing the proportion of received inventory expected to be sold at full price before markdown. Used to benchmark actual in-season performance and trigger markdown cadence decisions.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Assortment Planning; Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning

description: The date on which internal merchandise planning activities formally begin for this season, including OTB budget setting, assortment planning, and buy plan development in Anaplan and Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Precedes the trading start date by several months.
reference: Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: The date on which pre-production (PP) activities commence for this season, including fabric and trim approvals, fit samples, and pre-production sample sign-off. Key milestone in the T&A calendar tracked in Centric PLM and Infor PLM.
reference: Centric PLM — Collection Management; Infor PLM — BOM Management

description: The geographic region or market scope for which this season is defined (e.g., 'Global', 'North America', 'EMEA', 'APAC'). Supports multi-regional merchandise planning where season start/end dates and delivery windows may vary by market. Used in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan regional planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Master; Anaplan — Merchandise Financial Planning

description: Free-text narrative description of the season, capturing key strategic intent, collection positioning, target consumer profile, or planning notes. Used by merchandising and planning teams for context in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Master

description: Human-readable full name of the merchandise season (e.g., 'Spring/Summer 2025', 'Fall/Winter 2024', 'Holiday 2024', 'Resort 2025'). Used in reporting, planning dashboards, and buyer-facing documents.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Master

description: Current lifecycle state of the merchandise season. 'planning' indicates the season is in pre-production planning; 'active' indicates the season is currently trading; 'closed' indicates the season has ended trading; 'archived' indicates the season is retained for historical reference only. Controls visibility in planning tools and reporting.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Lifecycle

description: Classification of the trading season by industry-standard merchandise calendar type. Drives collection planning, OTB budget cycles, and markdown cadence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Spring/Summer|Fall/Winter|Resort|Pre-Fall|Holiday|Cruise — promote to reference product if additional season types are introduced]
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Classification; Apparel industry merchandise calendar standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this season record was sourced (e.g., ORM = Oracle Retail Merchandising System, ANAPLAN = Anaplan, CENTRIC = Centric PLM). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan; Centric PLM

description: The native season identifier as recorded in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Oracle Retail Merchandising System season number, Anaplan season key). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and source systems.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Master; Anaplan

description: The official first trading date of the merchandise season — the date on which the season's assortment becomes available for sale in retail stores and e-commerce channels. Serves as the temporal anchor for all season-level planning entities including assortment, OTB, allocation, and MFP.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Season Calendar

description: Reference code linking this season to its associated Time and Action (T&A) calendar template in Centric PLM or Infor PLM. The T&A calendar defines all critical path milestones from design brief through to final delivery for the season.
reference: Centric PLM — T&A Calendar; Infor PLM — Sourcing

description: The timestamp when this season record was last modified in the data platform. Used to detect changes, support incremental data loads, and maintain audit trail compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability

description: The target number of weeks of supply (WOS) to be maintained during the peak trading period of this season. Used to calibrate inventory depth in buy plans and allocation strategies, ensuring sufficient stock without excess inventory build-up.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Allocation; Anaplan — Demand Planning

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the size curve profile record in the merchandising system. Primary key for the size_curve data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — this is a planning reference resource defining expected unit sales distribution across size runs.

Foreign key linking to merchandising.channel. Business justification: Size curves vary by channel in apparel — DTC stores may have different size distributions than wholesale accounts. size_curve has a channel STRING attribute that is a denormalized reference to the cha

Foreign key linking to store.store_cluster. Business justification: Size curves in apparel are built per store cluster to reflect regional size demand patterns (e.g., larger sizes in certain regions). Planners assign size curves to clusters during buy planning. store_

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Segment-specific size curve derivation: size curves are built from sales data filtered by customer segment (e.g., petite vs. plus-size vs. athletic segments have distinct size distributions). Merchand

Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Size curves are defined at the merchandise hierarchy class/subclass level — a size curve for Women's Tops differs from one for Men's Bottoms. size_curve currently has class_code, department_code, and

Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Size curves are often season-specific (seasonal adjustment factors, basis periods); season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant

Foreign key linking to product.size_scale. Business justification: Size curves define percentage distributions across size ranges and must reference the actual size scale (XS-XXL, numeric, etc.) to be operationally meaningful. Allocation planning requires mapping cur

business_glossary_term: Sourcing Purchase Order Line Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order_line. Business justification: Size curves determine the size-run quantities on sourcing PO lines. Buyers apply the size curve to set ordered_quantity per size_code on each PO line. This link enables size curve accuracy reporting b

End date of the historical sell-through data period used to derive this size curve's distribution percentages. Together with basis_period_start_date, defines the historical window for statistical calibration of size ratios.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Start date of the historical sell-through data period used to derive this size curve's distribution percentages. Defines the lookback window for statistical calibration. Typically covers 1-3 prior seasons of comparable selling history.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Statistical confidence level of the size curve based on the volume and quality of historical sell-through data used in derivation. High confidence curves are used directly in buy plans; low confidence curves require planner override review. Drives curve applicability flags in Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Timestamp when this size curve record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) per platform conventions.

Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the size curve profile, used in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan for cross-system reference. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Method used to derive the size distribution percentages for this curve. Historical STR uses actual sell-through data; planner_override reflects manual planner judgment; vendor_recommended uses supplier-provided size ratios; statistical_model uses algorithmic forecasting; blended combines multiple methods. Affects confidence level assignment.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the size curve profile (e.g., 'Women's Fitted Tops Standard', 'Men's Denim 28-42 Bell Curve'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used in Oracle Retail and Anaplan planning interfaces.

Classification of the size curve profile indicating the fit and silhouette category that drives the distribution shape. Standard curves follow bell-curve distributions; fitted curves skew toward smaller sizes; oversized curves skew toward larger sizes. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|fitted|oversized|petite|plus|tall|slim|relaxed — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Centric PLM Collection Management

description: Date after which this size curve profile is no longer active for new planning runs. Null indicates an open-ended curve with no planned expiry. Used to manage curve versioning and seasonal transitions in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Date from which this size curve profile becomes effective for use in buy planning, allocation, and replenishment. Curves are versioned by effective date to support seasonal transitions and curve updates without disrupting in-flight planning cycles.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

business_glossary_term: Is Default Curve for Merchandise Class
description: Indicates whether this size curve is the default curve applied to new styles within the associated merchandise class when no style-specific curve exists. True = default fallback curve for the class. Used in Oracle Retail automated allocation and buy plan generation.

Indicates whether this size curve is approved for use in automated replenishment size pack calculations in Manhattan Associates WMS. True = curve can drive replenishment size breaks. False = curve is restricted to initial buy plan and allocation use only.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Date on which this size curve was last reviewed and validated by a merchandise planner. Curves not reviewed within a defined period (e.g., 2 seasons) are flagged for re-validation to ensure continued accuracy. Supports governance of curve quality.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Composite risk score (0.00-100.00) indicating the likelihood of size-related markdown exposure if this curve is applied to a buy plan. Derived from historical size fragmentation patterns and STR variance across sizes. Higher scores indicate greater markdown risk from size imbalance. Critical for GMROI optimization.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Free-text field for merchandise planner annotations regarding this size curve, including rationale for manual overrides, known anomalies in the historical basis data, or special instructions for application. Supports planning governance and knowledge transfer.

Serialized representation of the size distribution percentages for numeric size runs (waist sizes 28-42 or equivalent), stored as a delimited string of size:percentage pairs (e.g., '28:5.0|30:12.0|32:22.0|34:25.0|36:18.0|38:12.0|40:4.0|42:2.0'). Null for alpha or shoe size scale types. Used when the number of numeric sizes exceeds the available discrete percentage columns.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size L in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 = 25%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and replenishment size pack optimization.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size M in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 28.00 = 28%). Typically the modal size in standard bell-curve distributions. Null for numeric or shoe size scale types.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size S in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 18.00 = 18%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and allocation size breaks.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XL in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 14.00 = 14%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and initial distribution planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XS in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 8.50 = 8.5%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. All size distribution percentages must sum to 100.00 for a valid curve. Serves as the principal MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

business_glossary_term: Double Extra Large (XXL) Size Distribution Percentage
description: Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XXL in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 6.50 = 6.5%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Monitoring XXL distribution is critical for plus-size assortment planning and markdown minimization.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Geographic region for which this size curve applies (e.g., 'NA', 'EMEA', 'APAC', 'LATAM'). Size distributions vary significantly by region due to demographic differences. Used to apply region-specific size ratios in allocation and buy planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Name or user identifier of the merchandise planner who last reviewed and validated this size curve. Provides accountability for curve quality and supports audit trail requirements for merchandise planning governance.

Total number of units sold in the historical basis period used to derive this size curve. Higher sample counts yield higher statistical confidence. Used to validate confidence_level assignment — curves based on fewer than a threshold number of units are flagged as low confidence.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the base size distribution percentages to account for seasonal demand shifts (e.g., 1.0500 = 5% upward adjustment). Captures known seasonal size preference changes such as outerwear seasons skewing larger. Applied in Anaplan seasonal buy plan calculations.
reference: Anaplan Merchandise Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Serialized representation of the size distribution percentages for shoe size runs (US sizes 5-14 or equivalent), stored as a delimited string of size:percentage pairs (e.g., '7:8.0|7.5:10.0|8:15.0|8.5:18.0|9:17.0|9.5:14.0|10:12.0|10.5:6.0'). Null for alpha or numeric apparel size scale types.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Total number of discrete sizes in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., 6 for XS-XXL alpha, 8 for 28-42 even numeric). Drives the number of size break columns populated in allocation and replenishment size pack calculations.

Current lifecycle status of the size curve profile. Active curves are used in live buy plans and allocation runs. Draft curves are under development. Archived curves are retained for historical analysis but excluded from new planning cycles. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

The largest size in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., 'XXL', '42', '14'). Combined with size_range_min to define the full size run span. Used to validate that buy plan size breaks fall within the defined range.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; ISO 8559-1 Clothing Size Designation

description: The smallest size in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., 'XS', '28', '5'). Combined with size_range_max to define the full size run span. Used to validate that buy plan size breaks fall within the defined range.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; ISO 8559-1 Clothing Size Designation

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this size curve record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Oracle Retail is the primary system of record; Anaplan is used for planning-layer curves; Centric PLM may provide initial vendor-recommended curves.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning; Centric PLM

description: The minimum Sell-Through Rate (STR) achieved in the historical basis period for the data to be considered statistically valid for curve derivation. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 75.00 means 75% STR). Curves derived from low-STR periods may be flagged as low-confidence.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Timestamp when this size curve record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to any attribute of the size curve profile. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and for audit trail purposes.
Primary key for channel

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Channel-segment targeting: channel.target_customer_segment is a plain text denormalization. A proper FK enables merchandising systems to automatically apply segment-appropriate assortments, promotions

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Sales channels (DTC, wholesale, e-commerce) map to specific GL accounts for channel-level revenue recognition. Finance uses channel-GL mapping for channel P&L reporting and automated revenue account d
Reference to a parent channel if this channel is a sub-channel or variant within a hierarchical channel structure. Null for top-level channels.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Sales channels map to profit centers for channel-level P&L reporting. Finance tracks gross margin, AUR, and sell-through by channel via profit center assignment — essential for omnichannel profitabili
Reference to the geographic region where this channel primarily operates.
Numeric priority ranking (1=highest) used in merchandise allocation strategies when distributing inventory across channels.
Merchandising assortment strategy defining the breadth and depth of product selection offered through this channel.
Historical average transaction value in the channel's currency, used for financial planning and Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) analysis.
High-level categorization of the channel by operational medium: physical (brick-and-mortar), digital (online), or omnichannel (integrated).
Short alphanumeric code representing the channel, used for reporting and system integration (e.g., WEB, RETAIL, WHSL).
Detailed business description of the channel, including its purpose, target customer segment, and operational characteristics.
Full business name of the sales and distribution channel (e.g., E-Commerce, Retail Stores, Wholesale Partners).
Current operational status of the channel in the merchandising system lifecycle.
Classification of the channel based on ownership and operational model (direct-owned, indirect-partner, hybrid, franchise, marketplace, outlet).
Commission rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.0500 for 5%) paid to channel partners or sales associates for transactions in this channel.
Primary email address for business inquiries and operational communication related to this channel.
Primary phone number for business inquiries and operational communication related to this channel.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of operation for this channel.
Timestamp when this channel record was first created in the system.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial transactions in this channel.
Date when this channel ceased operations or was retired. Null for currently active channels.
Date when this channel became operational and available for transactions.
Primary method used to fulfill customer orders in this channel: ship from store, ship from distribution center, in-store pickup, drop-ship, or mixed.
Indicates whether real-time inventory levels are visible to customers in this channel (True) or not (False).
Indicates whether this channel record is currently active (True) or logically deleted/archived (False) in the system.
Timestamp when this channel record was last updated in the system.
Indicates whether purchases made through this channel are eligible for loyalty program points and rewards (True) or not (False).
Name of the business leader or manager responsible for this channel's performance and strategy.
Markdown policy governing price reductions in this channel to optimize sell-through rate (STR) and inventory turnover.
Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to this channel's operations or configuration.
Indicates whether this channel is integrated into the omnichannel customer experience (True) or operates independently (False).
Standard operating hours for this channel (e.g., 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM Mon-Sat, 24/7 for digital channels).
Comma-separated list of payment methods accepted in this channel (e.g., credit card, debit card, digital wallet, cash, gift card).
