description: Code identifying the geographic or channel-based price zone to which this price record belongs. Price zones allow differentiated pricing across markets (e.g., US_EAST, EU_WEST, APAC) within Oracle Retail Price Management.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Zone Management

description: The type of sales channel for which this price is applicable. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) retail, DTC e-commerce, wholesale, outlet, marketplace, or franchise. Enables channel-specific pricing strategy and margin analysis.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Channel Management; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this price record originated. ORM = Oracle Retail Merchandising, SAP_SD = SAP Sales & Distribution, SFCC = Salesforce Commerce Cloud, ANAPLAN = Anaplan Planning, MANUAL = manually entered. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP S/4HANA SD Module; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Indicates whether the MSRP and selling prices include applicable taxes (VAT, GST, sales tax). True = tax-inclusive pricing (common in EU/UK markets); False = tax-exclusive pricing (common in US markets). Governs POS and e-commerce price display.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — Tax Management; IFRS/GAAP — Revenue Recognition; GDPR — Consumer Pricing Transparency

description: The internal price used for inter-company or inter-division product transfers (e.g., from distribution center to retail store, or between legal entities). Used in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO for intercompany accounting and IFRS transfer pricing compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; IFRS/GAAP — Transfer Pricing; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines

description: Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the SKU. Used for POS (Point of Sale) scanning, RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tagging, and retail shelf management. Conforms to GS1 UPC-A or EAN-13 standards.
reference: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standard

description: Date and time when this price master record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management; ISO 9001 — Audit Trail Requirements

description: The applicable VAT (Value Added Tax) or GST rate percentage for this product in the specified country/region. Used for tax-inclusive price calculation, consumer receipt display, and regulatory tax reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — Tax Management; IFRS/GAAP — Tax Reporting; EU VAT Directive

description: The price at which the product is sold to wholesale partners, department stores, and distribution accounts. Used in wholesale margin analysis, partner negotiations, and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) reporting.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the promotion record. Primary key for the promotion data product in the merchandising domain.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD

description: Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Promotions are deployed to specific marketing audience segments (lapsed customers, loyalty tier, new acquires) distinct from CRM customer_segment_id. Marketing teams activate promotions via audience s

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Merchandising promotions (markdowns, discounts) are executed as part of marketing campaigns. This link tracks which campaign drives each pricing action, enabling integrated promotional planning and RO

Foreign key linking to store.store_cluster. Business justification: Promotions in apparel are commonly scoped to store clusters (e.g., outlet cluster clearance events, flagship cluster VIP promotions). Cluster-level promotion targeting is a standard named process in a

Foreign key linking to product.collection. Business justification: Collection-level promotions are a standard apparel retail practice — end-of-season collection sales, new collection launch promotions. This FK enables promotion scoping by collection, collection markd

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotion costs (advertising, discounts, loyalty rewards) are charged to marketing/merchandising cost centers. Finance requires this link to allocate promotional expenses and track cost center budget

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Promotions target customer segments beyond loyalty programs (student discounts, military offers, first-time buyer incentives). Segment-specific promotions drive acquisition and retention strategies wi

Foreign key linking to marketing.event. Business justification: Promotional events (flash sales, trunk shows, end-of-season events) activate specific promotions. Merchandising teams track promotional lift per marketing event for markdown budget accountability. A f

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Promotions involve markdown budgets and discount costs posted to specific GL accounts (markdown expense, promotional allowance). Finance requires GL account assignment on promotions for promotional co

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Promotional markdown costs are accrued in a specific accounting ledger. Finance requires ledger assignment on promotions to post markdown expense accruals to the correct ledger for period-close and to

Reference to the loyalty program associated with loyalty-linked promotions. Null for non-loyalty promotion types. Links to CRM (Customer Relationship Management) loyalty program master data in Adobe Experience Platform or Salesforce.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform; Salesforce CRM; GDPR Article 6 (Lawful Basis for Processing)

description: Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Promotions have department_id, class_id but no FK to merchandise_hierarchy master. Adding merchandise_hierarchy_id FK normalizes merchandise classification and removes redundant hierarchy attributes.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Promotions impact margin by channel/brand, tracked via profit centers for ROI analysis. Financial reporting requires linking promotional lift and cost to profit centers to measure promotional effectiv

Foreign key linking to marketing.promo_code. Business justification: Marketing promo codes trigger merchandising promotions at checkout. This link connects the code distribution mechanism (marketing) to the discount rules and budget impact (merchandising). Essential fo

Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Promotions are seasonal events (Spring Sale, Holiday Clearance); season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant season_code. FK wi

Original promotion identifier as recorded in the source operational system (e.g., Oracle Retail promotion number, SAP SD condition record number). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and source systems of record.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD Condition Records

description: Realized percentage increase in unit sales or revenue achieved by this promotion compared to baseline, captured post-promotion from SAP CAR POS data and Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Compared against planned_lift_pct for STR (Sell-Through Rate) analysis and future promotion planning.
reference: SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR); Anaplan Financial Planning

description: Realized gross revenue generated during the promotion period, sourced from SAP CAR POS transactions and Salesforce Commerce Cloud order data. Compared against planned_revenue for post-promotion performance analysis and GMROI optimization.
reference: SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR); Salesforce Commerce Cloud; IFRS 15

description: Realized number of units sold during the promotion period, sourced from SAP CAR POS and Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Used in STR (Sell-Through Rate) analysis and post-promotion inventory reconciliation.
reference: SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR); Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Workflow approval state of the promotion record within the merchandising governance process. Pending: awaiting review; Approved: authorized by merchandising director or finance; Rejected: not approved; Escalated: referred to senior leadership. Distinct from promotion_status which tracks operational lifecycle.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD Workflow

description: Name or employee identifier of the merchandising manager or director who authorized this promotion. Supports audit trail requirements for pricing governance and FTC compliance on promotional advertising claims.
reference: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) - Promotional Advertising Guidelines; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Date and time when the promotion was formally approved by the authorized merchandising stakeholder. Supports audit trail and compliance with FTC promotional advertising disclosure requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) - Promotional Advertising Guidelines; Oracle Retail

description: Sales channel(s) where the promotion is valid. Retail_store: physical POS via SAP CAR; ecommerce: digital storefront via Salesforce Commerce Cloud; wholesale: B2B distribution partners; dtc: Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) channels; all: applicable across all channels. Drives channel-specific promotional lift analysis.
reference: SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR); Salesforce Commerce Cloud; Oracle Retail

description: Alphanumeric coupon or voucher code that customers enter at POS or e-commerce checkout to activate the promotion when coupon_required is True (e.g., SAVE20, LOYALTY10). Null when coupon_required is False.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR)

description: Indicates whether the customer must present or enter a coupon code to redeem the promotion. True = coupon code required at POS or e-commerce checkout; False = automatic/stackable promotion applied without customer action.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD

description: Date and time when the promotion record was first created in the source system (Oracle Retail or SAP SD). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR/CCPA data governance requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); CCPA; Oracle Retail

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code applicable to all monetary fields on this promotion record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international retail and wholesale channels.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Markdown (MD) percentage applied to the original MSRP or AUR (Average Unit Retail) for percentage-off promotion types. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 = 25% off). Null for non-percentage promotion types such as fixed_price or bogo. Used in IMU/MMU (Initial Markup/Maintained Markup) impact analysis.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Calendar date on which the promotion expires and is no longer applicable. Nullable for open-ended evergreen promotions. Used to calculate WOS (Weeks of Supply) impact and OTB (Open-to-Buy) planning in Anaplan.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Absolute promotional selling price applied to eligible items for fixed_price promotion types (e.g., $29.99). Null for percentage-off or BOGO mechanics. Represents the promotional AUR (Average Unit Retail) override in Oracle Retail Price Management.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management; SAP SD Condition Type PR00

description: Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the promotion is valid (e.g., USA|CAN|GBR). Null = global applicability. Supports multi-market promotional planning and compliance with regional consumer protection regulations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; FTC Promotional Guidelines; GDPR

business_glossary_term: Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) Impact
description: Estimated or realized GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) impact of the promotion, calculated as gross margin dollars generated per dollar of inventory investment during the promotional period. A core KPI in apparel merchandising financial planning. Classified confidential as it reveals margin strategy.
reference: Anaplan Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System; IFRS/GAAP

description: Indicates whether this promotion is exclusively available to loyalty program members. True = restricted to loyalty members only (tracked via Adobe Experience Platform or CRM); False = available to all customers. Supports CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) and loyalty program ROI analysis.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform; Salesforce CRM; GDPR Article 6

description: Date and time when the promotion record was most recently modified in the source system. Tracks changes to promotion terms, dates, or discount mechanics for audit and data lineage purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); CCPA; Oracle Retail

description: Allocated markdown (MD) budget for this promotion, representing the maximum planned reduction in gross margin approved by the merchandising team. Expressed in currency_code. Managed in Anaplan OTB (Open-to-Buy) financial planning. Classified confidential as it reflects internal pricing strategy.
reference: Anaplan Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Price Management; IFRS/GAAP

description: Level of the merchandise hierarchy to which the promotion applies. Determines eligible SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) in Oracle Retail: all_merchandise (storewide), department (e.g., Footwear), class (e.g., Running Shoes), subclass (e.g., Men's Running), sku (specific SKU), style (all colorways of a style). Drives assortment planning and allocation strategies.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Oracle Retail Assortment Planning

description: Minimum transaction value (in local currency) a customer must reach to qualify for the promotion. Used for threshold-based promotions (e.g., spend $100 get 20% off). Supports AOV (Average Order Value) uplift strategies in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and SAP CAR.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD

description: Minimum number of units a customer must purchase to qualify for the promotion. Relevant for BOGO, bundle, and quantity-threshold promotions. Aligns with MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) concepts in wholesale channel promotions.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD

description: Forecasted percentage increase in unit sales or revenue expected from this promotion compared to baseline, as planned in Anaplan merchandise financial planning. Used to set OTB (Open-to-Buy) budgets and evaluate GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) impact pre-promotion.
reference: Anaplan Demand Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Forecasted gross revenue expected to be generated during the promotion period, as modeled in Anaplan merchandise financial planning. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. Used for OTB budget setting and GMROI planning.
reference: Anaplan Financial Planning; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Forecasted number of units expected to be sold during the promotion period, as planned in Anaplan demand planning. Used to set inventory allocation targets and ATS (Available to Sell) thresholds in Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: Anaplan Demand Planning; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple non-stackable promotions apply to the same transaction or item. Lower number = higher priority. Managed in Oracle Retail Price Management and SAP SD condition access sequences.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management; SAP SD Condition Access Sequence

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code used to identify and reference the promotion across Oracle Retail, SAP SD, and customer-facing channels (e.g., SUMMER25, BOGO-DENIM). Used in EDI transactions, POS systems, and e-commerce storefronts.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD Module SD-BF (Pricing and Conditions)

description: Free-text business description of the promotion's terms, conditions, and marketing narrative as communicated to customers and store associates (e.g., 'Get 25% off all women's outerwear this weekend only'). Used in POS displays, e-commerce banners, and marketing collateral.
reference: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) - Promotional Advertising Disclosure Requirements; Oracle Retail

description: Human-readable business name of the promotion as displayed in merchandising systems and marketing materials (e.g., 'Summer Clearance 2025', 'Buy One Get One Denim'). Used in Oracle Retail and Anaplan merchandise financial planning.

Current lifecycle state of the promotion record within Oracle Retail and SAP SD workflow. Draft: under construction; Approved: authorized by merchandising; Active: currently running; Paused: temporarily suspended; Expired: past end date; Cancelled: terminated before activation.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD

description: Categorical classification of the promotion's discount mechanic. Drives how the discount is calculated at POS and in e-commerce. Values: percentage_off (e.g., 20% off), fixed_price (e.g., $29.99), bogo (Buy One Get One), bundle (multi-item offer), loyalty (loyalty program linked reduction), clearance (end-of-season markdown). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage_off|fixed_price|bogo|bundle|loyalty|clearance|gift_with_purchase|threshold_discount — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP SD Condition Types

description: Percentage of available promotional inventory sold during the promotion period (units sold / units available × 100). A key KPI in apparel merchandising for evaluating promotion effectiveness and informing future markdown (MD) and clearance strategies. Sourced post-promotion from Oracle Retail and SAP CAR.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR)

description: Operational system of record from which this promotion record originated. Oracle_retail: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; sap_sd: SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution; salesforce_cc: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; anaplan: Anaplan planning platform; manual: manually entered. Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; SAP S/4HANA SD; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Indicates whether this promotion can be combined with other active promotions on the same transaction. True = can stack with other promotions; False = exclusive promotion, cannot be combined. Critical for margin protection and MMU (Maintained Markup) management.
reference: Oracle Retail Price Management; SAP SD Condition Exclusion Groups

description: Calendar date on which the promotion becomes effective and eligible for application at POS, e-commerce, and wholesale channels. Aligns with the T&A (Time and Action) calendar in Anaplan merchandise planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Anaplan Merchandise Planning

description: Maximum number of times a single customer can redeem this promotion. Null = unlimited per customer. Enforced at POS via SAP CAR and at e-commerce checkout via Salesforce Commerce Cloud to prevent abuse.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR)

description: Maximum total number of times this promotion can be redeemed across all customers and transactions. Null = unlimited redemptions. Used for limited-time flash sales and exclusive loyalty offers to control promotional liability.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Unique identifier for the cost sheet record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to product.bom. Business justification: A cost sheet in apparel is derived from or validated against a Bill of Materials — the BOM defines the material quantities and unit costs that feed into the cost sheet's fabric_cost, material_cost, an

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Cost sheets are prepared per legal entity in multi-entity apparel groups, determining currency, tax jurisdiction, and transfer pricing treatment. Company code on cost_sheet is required for correct sta

Foreign key linking to logistics.duty_calculation. Business justification: Cost sheets incorporate estimated landed duty costs during product development. Linking to actual duty calculations enables variance analysis between estimated and actual duty, improving costing accur

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Cost sheets drive GL account determination for COGS, freight, duty, and overhead postings in apparel costing. Finance teams use cost sheet GL mapping to ensure correct account assignment when cost she

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Multi-GAAP apparel groups maintain parallel cost sheets per accounting ledger (IFRS vs local GAAP). Linking cost_sheet to ledger enables correct valuation method application and parallel accounting fo

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Cost sheets are prepared for specific profit center contexts (market/channel pricing strategies). Essential for calculating landed costs and target margins by segment, supporting pricing decisions in
Reference to the season or collection for which this cost sheet applies.
Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) if cost sheet is SKU-level rather than style-level.
Reference to the style or product design for which this cost sheet is prepared.

Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Cost sheets in apparel are negotiated at factory level — CMT and labor costs vary by factory. Sourcing teams compare factory-level cost sheets for vendor selection and margin analysis. The existing pr

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Cost sheets are negotiated with a vendor legal entity for payment terms, currency, and vendor performance reporting. Finance and sourcing teams need vendor-level cost sheet aggregation for spend analy

Foreign key linking to customer.wholesale_account. Business justification: Cost sheets in apparel are frequently prepared per wholesale account because each account has negotiated incoterms, payment terms, and destination-specific duty rates. Finance and sales ops teams gene
Actual Cost of Goods Sold realized after production and delivery, used for financial reporting and variance analysis.
Date when the cost sheet was officially approved for use in production planning and financial forecasting.
Cut, Make, and Trim cost representing the labor and manufacturing service charges for cutting fabric, assembling garment, and finishing operations.
Business identifier or document number for the cost sheet, used for external reference and tracking.
Current approval and workflow status of the cost sheet within the PLM and costing approval process.
Stage in the costing lifecycle indicating the maturity and accuracy of the cost data (e.g., initial estimate during design, final costing before production, actual costing post-production).
Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the product is manufactured, used for customs and compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3, Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Textile Labeling

description: Timestamp when the cost sheet record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all cost amounts are denominated.
Three-letter ISO country code representing the destination market or country of import for this cost sheet.
Date from which this cost sheet becomes effective and applicable for production orders and financial planning.
Date when this cost sheet expires and is no longer valid, typically due to material cost changes or new negotiations.
Cost of fabric materials used in the garment or product, subset of total material cost.
Free On Board cost representing the total cost of the product at the port of origin, including manufacturing and local logistics but excluding international freight and duties.
International freight and shipping cost from origin port to destination port or warehouse.
Harmonized System code used for international trade classification and customs duty determination.
Initial Markup percentage calculated as (MSRP - COGS) / MSRP, representing the planned gross margin at initial retail price.

International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining the responsibilities and costs between buyer and seller for shipping and delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Direct labor cost for manufacturing operations, may overlap with or be part of CMT cost depending on costing methodology.
reference: Infor PLM Costing Module, SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Landed Duty Paid cost representing the total delivered cost including FOB, freight, duties, and all logistics costs to the destination warehouse.
Production lead time in days from order placement to delivery, associated with this cost sheet.
Total cost of all raw materials including fabrics, trims, accessories, and packaging materials used in the product.
reference: Infor PLM Costing Module, SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Timestamp when the cost sheet record was last modified or updated.
Minimum Order Quantity required by the factory or supplier for this cost sheet to be valid.
Manufacturers Suggested Retail Price for the product, used to calculate Initial Markup (IMU) and margin targets.
Free-text notes and comments regarding the cost sheet, including assumptions, special conditions, or variance explanations.
Manufacturing overhead and indirect costs allocated to the product including factory utilities, supervision, and facility costs.
Cost of packaging materials including polybags, hangtags, cartons, and shipping materials.
Payment terms negotiated with the factory or supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Letter of Credit).
Target Cost of Goods Sold established by merchandising and finance teams to achieve desired margin objectives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO, Merchandising Financial Planning

description: Target gross margin percentage established by merchandising and finance for this product or style.
Cost of trims and accessories such as buttons, zippers, labels, threads, and embellishments.
Version number or identifier for the cost sheet, allowing tracking of revisions and updates over time.
Wholesale selling price to retail partners and distributors, distinct from MSRP and used for B2B channel pricing.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System, SAP S/4HANA SD

business_glossary_term: Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) Price ID
description: Unique identifier for the MSRP price record. Primary key for the MSRP price entity.

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: MSRP prices are legal-entity-specific in multi-country apparel groups due to VAT, transfer pricing, and local market regulations. Company code on msrp_price ensures correct currency, tax treatment, an

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Segment-specific pricing is standard in apparel — loyalty member pricing, student pricing, and VIP segment pricing are distinct MSRP strategies. Price management teams create segment-differentiated pr
Reference to the SKU for which this MSRP price is defined. Links to the product SKU master.
Reference to the style for which this MSRP price is defined. Used when pricing is set at style level rather than SKU level.
Current approval workflow status of the MSRP price record. Tracks the price through the approval process before activation.
Date and time when this MSRP price was approved. Part of the approval workflow audit trail.
The target average unit retail price expected to be achieved after markdowns and promotions. Used for financial planning and Open-to-Buy (OTB) calculations.
Indicates which sales channels this MSRP applies to: Direct-to-Consumer (DTC), wholesale, retail stores, or e-commerce. Supports multi-channel pricing strategies.
Reference price from competitor analysis used to inform MSRP positioning. Supports competitive pricing strategy and market positioning.
The cost of goods sold (COGS) or landed cost used as the basis for calculating the initial markup percentage. Business-confidential financial data.
Date and time when this MSRP price record was first created in the system. Part of the record lifecycle audit trail.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the MSRP amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY, JPY).
The date on which this MSRP price expires and is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended price with no planned expiration.
The date from which this MSRP price becomes active and valid for use in pricing and merchandising systems.
The initial markup percentage calculated as the difference between MSRP and cost, expressed as a percentage of MSRP. Key metric for profitability planning.
Date and time when this MSRP price record was last updated. Part of the record lifecycle audit trail.
Three-letter ISO country code representing the market or region where this MSRP applies (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, DEU, CHN).
The minimum price at which retailers are allowed to advertise this product. Used to protect brand positioning and prevent price erosion in wholesale channels.
The suggested retail price amount set by the manufacturer for this SKU or style in the specified market and currency. This is the recommended selling price to consumers.
Free-text notes or comments about this MSRP price record. Used for documenting special pricing considerations, exceptions, or business context.

The business reason for creating or updating this MSRP record. Supports price change audit trail and analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_product|cost_change|market_adjustment|competitive_response|seasonal|promotional|correction — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The pricing strategy classification for this MSRP: penetration (low to gain share), premium (high for exclusivity), competitive (market-aligned), value (cost-plus), or psychological (charm pricing).
Classification of the product price positioning within the assortment (good/better/best framework). Used for merchandising and assortment planning.
Geographic or market-based pricing zone code used for regional price differentiation within a country or market. Supports localized pricing strategies.
Suggested markdown percentage for promotional or clearance pricing. Used for planning maintained markup (MMU) and sell-through rate (STR) targets.
Code representing the fashion season or collection period for which this MSRP is set (e.g., SS24, FW24, HOLIDAY24). Links pricing to merchandising calendar.
The unique identifier of this MSRP price record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
The operational system of record from which this MSRP price originated (Oracle Retail Price Management, SAP S/4HANA SD, Centric PLM, manual entry, or Anaplan).
Indicates whether the MSRP amount includes VAT or other consumption taxes. True if tax-inclusive, false if tax-exclusive.
The price at which the product is sold to wholesale partners or distributors. Typically lower than MSRP to allow retailer margin. Business-confidential.
Unique identifier for the bill of materials record. Primary key for the BOM product.
Reference to the specific colorway variant of the style. Links to the colorway master product.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: BOM development and costing activities are performed by product development or technical design departments tracked as cost centers. Necessary for allocating pre-production costs and tracking departme

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: BOMs determine material cost GL account assignments for standard costing and variance analysis. Apparel cost accountants require BOM-to-GL mapping to post material costs, CMT costs, and duty costs to

Foreign key linking to logistics.hs_code. Business justification: BOMs determine material composition and country of origin, which drive HS classification and duty rates. Linking BOM to HS code enables accurate costing, duty estimation, and trade compliance validati
Reference to the merchandising season for which this BOM is designed. Links to the season master product.
Reference to the parent style for which this BOM is defined. Links to the style master product.

Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: BOMs in apparel are factory-specific — construction methods, CMT costs, and material sourcing vary by factory. The same style may have different BOMs for different factories. The existing bom.vendor_i
Reference to the primary supplier or factory responsible for manufacturing this product. Links to the supplier master product.
Business identifier for the BOM, typically assigned by the PLM system. Used for external communication with suppliers and factories.
Current lifecycle status of the BOM indicating its approval state and readiness for production use.
Classification of the BOM by its intended use case: engineering BOM for design, manufacturing BOM for production, costing BOM for financial analysis, or planning BOM for procurement.
Total labor and manufacturing cost charged by the factory for cutting, making, and trimming the garment. Does not include material costs.
Approval status of the cost buildup by finance or merchandising teams. Approved costs are used for margin analysis and pricing decisions.
Date when the cost buildup was approved. Used for audit trail and version control.
Version identifier for the cost buildup to track changes in costing assumptions, material prices, or factory quotes over time.

Stage of the costing process indicating the maturity and accuracy of cost data: initial estimate during design, preliminary costing after sampling, final costing before production, or actual costing post-production.
Country where the product is manufactured. Used for customs documentation, duty calculation, and trade compliance. Three-letter ISO country code.
Timestamp when this BOM record was first created in the PLM system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.
Three-letter ISO currency code for all cost fields in this BOM record. Supports multi-currency costing for global sourcing.
Labor cost component for cutting fabric and materials. Part of the CMT cost breakdown.
Import duty cost per unit calculated by applying the duty rate to the FOB cost. Varies by destination country and trade agreements.
Import duty rate percentage applied to the product based on its HS Code and country of origin. Used to calculate duty cost.
Date from which this BOM version becomes active and valid for production or costing purposes.
Date after which this BOM version is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended BOM with no planned obsolescence.
Detailed breakdown of fabric composition by material type and percentage (e.g., 60% Cotton, 40% Polyester). Required for FTC labeling compliance and OEKO-TEX certification.
reference: FTC Textile Labeling Requirements, OEKO-TEX Standard 100

description: Total cost of the product at the port of origin, including materials, CMT, and factory overhead. Excludes freight, duty, and landed costs.
Transportation cost per unit to ship the product from the factory to the destination warehouse or distribution center.
Initial markup percentage calculated as (MSRP - LDP Cost) / MSRP. Key profitability metric for merchandising and pricing decisions.
Total landed cost per unit including FOB, freight, duty, and all other costs to deliver the product to the destination warehouse. Represents the true COGS.
Number of days required from purchase order placement to product delivery at the destination warehouse. Used for supply chain planning and TNA calendar.
Labor cost component for sewing and assembling the garment. Part of the CMT cost breakdown.
User identifier or name of the person who last modified this BOM record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.
Timestamp when this BOM record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

Unique identifier for the supplier audit finding record. Primary key for the supplier audit finding entity.
Reference to the parent compliance or quality audit during which this finding was identified.
Actual date when the corrective action was completed, verified, and the finding was formally closed. Null if the finding remains open.
Additional notes, observations, or context provided by the auditor regarding the finding, including mitigating factors, supplier cooperation, or special circumstances.
Description of the potential or actual business impact of the finding, including production delays, quality issues, regulatory penalties, reputational risk, or supply chain disruption.

Current status of the finding in the corrective action lifecycle. Open: newly identified, action not started. In Progress: corrective action underway. Pending Verification: action completed, awaiting verification. Closed: verified and accepted. Overdue: past target closure date without completion.
Detailed description of the corrective action plan (CAP) required to remediate the finding, including specific steps, process changes, training requirements, or infrastructure improvements needed.
reference: ISO 9001 - Quality Management Systems, WRAP Principles

description: Estimated or actual cost incurred by the supplier to implement the corrective action and remediate the finding, including infrastructure upgrades, training, process changes, or equipment purchases.
Timestamp when this supplier audit finding record was first created in the system, following the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost to remediate amount, such as USD, EUR, CNY, or INR.
Date when the finding was escalated to higher management levels or external stakeholders for intervention or decision-making.

Boolean indicator (True/False) identifying whether this finding has been escalated to senior management, legal, or executive leadership due to severity, regulatory risk, or failure to remediate within agreed timelines.
URL or file path to supporting evidence documentation for the finding, including photographs, audit reports, corrective action plans, or verification records stored in the document management system.

Primary classification of the non-conformance finding based on the compliance domain. Categories include Labor (working conditions, child labor, forced labor), Health & Safety (workplace safety, PPE, ergonomics), Environmental (waste management, emissions, water usage), Chemical (restricted substances, OEKO-TEX compliance), Fire Safety (exits, extinguishers, evacuation plans), and Wages (minimum wage, overtime pay, payroll records).
reference: WRAP Principles, FLA Workplace Code of Conduct, OEKO-TEX Standard 100

description: Detailed narrative description of the non-conformance finding, including specific observations, conditions found, and evidence collected during the audit.
reference: ISO 19011 - Guidelines for Auditing Management Systems

description: Date when the non-conformance finding was first identified during the audit inspection or assessment.
Business-readable unique reference number assigned to this non-conformance finding for tracking and communication purposes.
Detailed subcategory or specific compliance area within the primary finding category, providing granular classification for analysis and reporting.
