Current lifecycle status of the vendor contact record. Indicates whether the contact is actively engaged, temporarily unavailable, or no longer with the vendor organization.
Timestamp when this vendor contact record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.
Department or functional area within the vendor organization where the contact works (e.g., Sales, Quality Assurance, Compliance, Logistics, Finance, Production).
Date when this contact person's role ended or they ceased to be the designated contact. Null if the contact is currently active. Used for contact history tracking and audit trails.
Date when this contact person began their role with the vendor or became the designated contact for this business relationship. Used for contact history tracking and audit trails.
Primary business email address of the vendor contact person. Used for official communications, purchase orders, and compliance documentation.
Emergency contact phone number for urgent situations requiring immediate vendor response, such as production issues, quality failures, or compliance incidents.
reference: GDPR Article 4, CCPA Section 1798.140, ITU-T E.164

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this contact is currently active and should be used for communications. True if active, False if inactive or no longer with the vendor organization.
Boolean flag indicating whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the vendor. True if primary, False otherwise. Only one primary contact should exist per vendor per role type.
Official job title or position of the contact within the vendor organization (e.g., Account Manager, Quality Control Manager, Compliance Officer, Production Coordinator).

Primary language(s) spoken by the contact for business communications. Supports multilingual vendor management and reduces miscommunication risk. Use ISO 639-1 two-letter codes (e.g., EN, ZH, ES, FR, IT, PT, DE, JA, KO, VI, BN, HI).
Date of the most recent communication or interaction with this vendor contact. Used for relationship management and engagement tracking.
Timestamp when this vendor contact record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trails, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.
Mobile phone number of the vendor contact person for urgent communications and field coordination.
reference: GDPR Article 4, CCPA Section 1798.140, ITU-T E.164

description: Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the vendor contact. May include communication preferences, special instructions, relationship history, or other relevant information.
Physical office location or site where the contact is based within the vendor organization (e.g., factory name, office branch, city). Used for site visit planning and logistics coordination.
Primary business phone number of the vendor contact person, including country code and extension if applicable.
reference: GDPR Article 4, CCPA Section 1798.140, ITU-T E.164

description: The contact's preferred method of communication for routine business interactions. Used to optimize communication effectiveness and response times.
Name of the contact's direct manager or supervisor within the vendor organization. Used for escalation paths and organizational context.

value_regex: Account Manager|QC Liaison|Compliance Officer|Logistics Coordinator|Finance Contact|Production Manager
description: Standardized role classification indicating the primary function of the contact in the vendor relationship. Used for routing communications and workflow assignments.

Specific area of expertise or specialization of the contact within their role (e.g., Fabric Sourcing, Garment Construction, Chemical Compliance, Export Documentation, Quality Testing). Used for routing specialized inquiries.

Time zone of the vendor contact's primary work location. Used for scheduling meetings and coordinating communications across global supply chains. Use IANA time zone database format (e.g., Asia/Shanghai, America/New_York, Europe/London).
WeChat contact identifier or username used for instant messaging communication with the vendor contact, commonly used for China-based suppliers.
WhatsApp contact identifier (typically phone number) used for instant messaging communication with the vendor contact, commonly used in international sourcing operations.
Number of years of professional experience the contact has in their field or role. Used for assessing vendor capability and relationship strength.
Unique identifier for the bank account record. Primary key for the bank account master data entity.
Internal account identifier within the house bank structure. Used in combination with house_bank_id to uniquely identify the account in SAP.

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Bank accounts are linked to GL accounts for reconciliation and financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code
Self-referencing FK on bank_account (cash_pool_header_bank_account_id)

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Bank accounts belong to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.
Date when the bank account was officially closed. Null for active accounts.
Legal name of the account holder as registered with the bank. Typically matches the company legal entity name.
The actual bank account number as assigned by the financial institution. Format varies by country and banking system.
Date when the bank account was officially opened and activated with the financial institution.
Free-text description of the business purpose and usage of this bank account. Provides context for treasury management and audit purposes.

Current operational status of the bank account. Active accounts are in use, inactive are temporarily suspended, closed are permanently terminated, blocked are restricted by bank or company, dormant have no recent activity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Master Data Management

description: Classification of the bank account based on its business purpose. Operating accounts handle day-to-day transactions, payroll accounts for employee payments, Letter of Credit (LC) accounts for trade finance, cash pooling for treasury optimization, escrow for held funds, and transit for in-process payments.
Available balance after accounting for holds, pending transactions, and reserved funds. Represents actual spendable cash.
First line of the bank branch physical address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Second line of the bank branch physical address (suite, floor, building). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Name of the specific bank branch where the account is maintained.
City where the bank branch is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Country-specific bank identifier code (e.g., routing number in USA, sort code in UK, bank code in other countries). Used for domestic payment processing.
Full legal name of the financial institution where the account is held.
Primary contact phone number for the bank branch. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Postal or ZIP code of the bank branch location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
State or province where the bank branch is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Flag indicating whether this account participates in a cash pooling arrangement for treasury optimization across multiple entities or accounts.
Three-letter ISO country code where the bank account is domiciled. Important for regulatory reporting and tax compliance.
Timestamp when this bank account master record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency denomination of the bank account.
Current available balance in the bank account as of the last reconciliation or statement date. Used for cash flow management and liquidity analysis.
Unique identifier for the house bank in SAP S/4HANA FI. Represents the company's banking partner where the account is maintained.
International Bank Account Number used for cross-border payments and international transactions. Standardized format for global banking.
Annual interest rate percentage applied to the account balance. May be positive for interest-bearing accounts or negative for certain treasury accounts.
Timestamp when this bank account master record was last updated in the system.
Date when the last bank reconciliation was completed for this account. Critical for End of Month (EOM) close and financial reporting accuracy.
Date of the most recent bank statement received and processed for this account. Used for reconciliation tracking.
Flag indicating whether this account is designated for Letter of Credit (LC) issuance and settlement. Critical for apparel fashion global sourcing and Free on Board (FOB) payment terms.
reference: UCP 600 Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits

description: Maximum Letter of Credit facility limit available on this account. Used for managing trade finance capacity for international supplier payments.
reference: UCP 600 Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits

description: Minimum balance requirement set by the bank or company policy to avoid fees or maintain account status.
Maximum overdraft or credit facility limit extended by the bank for this account. Zero if no overdraft facility exists.
Comma-separated list of payment methods supported by this account (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check, EDI, SWIFT). Used for payment run configuration.
Email address of the bank relationship manager. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Name of the bank relationship manager or account officer responsible for this account. Key contact for banking services and issue resolution.
Phone number of the bank relationship manager. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

business_glossary_term: Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) Bank Identifier Code (BIC)
description: SWIFT/BIC code identifying the bank and branch for international wire transfers and Letter of Credit (LC) transactions.
Unique identifier for the company code record. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_RESOURCE (legal entity as a managed organizational resource).

Primary accounting standard applied for statutory financial reporting. Options include IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), US GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), local GAAP, or hybrid approaches for dual reporting.
Date on which the company code was activated in the SAP S/4HANA FI system and began posting financial transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Configuration

description: First line of the registered legal address for the company code. Includes street number and street name.
Second line of the registered legal address for the company code. Includes building name, suite, or floor information.
Primary business segment classification for this company code within the apparel fashion group. Used for segment reporting, profitability analysis, and strategic planning.
Four-character code identifying the chart of accounts assigned to this company code. The COA defines the structure of General Ledger (GL) accounts used for recording financial transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

description: City or municipality of the registered legal address for the company code.

Four-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the legal entity within the SAP S/4HANA FI system. This is the business identifier used across all financial transactions and statutory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Configuration

description: Full legal name of the company as registered with statutory authorities. Used for financial statements, tax filings, and legal documentation.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 - Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Abbreviated or trading name of the company used for internal reporting and operational displays.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master Data

description: Current operational status of the company code. Active indicates the entity is operational and posting transactions. Inactive, dormant, liquidation, or merged indicate non-operational states.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Status

description: Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code is included in group-level financial consolidation. True indicates the entity participates in consolidated financial statements.
Code identifying the consolidation group or reporting entity to which this company code belongs. Used for multi-level consolidation hierarchies and segment reporting.

Four-character code identifying the Controlling (CO) area to which this company code is assigned. The controlling area is the organizational unit for cost accounting, profitability analysis, and internal management reporting.

Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code uses cost-of-sales accounting (income statement by function) or period accounting (income statement by nature). True indicates cost-of-sales method.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 - Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of domicile for the legal entity. Determines tax jurisdiction, statutory reporting requirements, and local GAAP applicability.
Timestamp indicating when this company code record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Four-character code identifying the credit control area responsible for managing customer credit limits and credit exposure for this company code.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the local currency of the company code. All financial transactions for this entity are recorded in this currency before consolidation.
Date on which the company code was deactivated or ceased operations. Null if the entity is still active.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Configuration

description: Primary email address for official correspondence with the company code.
Fax number for the company code, if applicable. Used for formal legal and regulatory correspondence.
Four-character code defining the field status variant that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during financial document posting for this company code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Field Status Configuration

description: Two-character code defining the fiscal year structure for this company code, including the number of posting periods, special periods for year-end adjustments, and fiscal year start/end dates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant Configuration

description: Geographic region classification for this company code. Used for regional performance analysis, geographic segment reporting, and market-based strategic decisions.
Date on which the legal entity was incorporated or registered with the statutory authority. Used for legal compliance and entity lifecycle tracking.
Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code participates in automated intercompany clearing and reconciliation processes. True indicates intercompany transactions are tracked and cleared.
Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language used for financial reporting and communication for this company code.
User ID or username of the person who last modified this company code record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp indicating when this company code record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Primary contact phone number for the company code headquarters or registered office.
Postal or ZIP code of the registered legal address for the company code.
Four-character code defining the posting period variant that controls which posting periods are open or closed for transaction posting in this company code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period Control

description: Code identifying the standard profit center hierarchy used for profitability analysis and internal management reporting for this company code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center Accounting

description: State, province, or administrative region of the registered legal address for the company code.
Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to this company code. Determines tax calculation rules, tax reporting requirements, and compliance with local tax authorities.
IANA time zone identifier for the company code location. Used for timestamp normalization and cross-entity transaction reconciliation.
VAT or sales tax registration number assigned by the local tax authority. Required for VAT reporting and cross-border transactions within VAT jurisdictions.
Unique identifier for the cost center record. Primary key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Cost centers belong to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Cost centers can be linked to profit centers for profitability analysis. Normalizing STRING profit_center_number to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profit_center
The primary activity type or cost driver associated with this cost center (e.g., 'Machine Hours', 'Labor Hours', 'Square Footage', 'Headcount'). Used for activity-based costing and allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Activity Type Master

description: The cumulative actual costs posted to this cost center from the beginning of the fiscal year to the current period. Used for budget variance and EOM close reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Actual Line Items

description: The identifier of the allocation cycle in which this cost center participates as sender or receiver. Used for overhead distribution and internal cost allocation.
The brand or label to which this cost center is aligned (e.g., 'MAIN', 'PREMIUM', 'OUTLET'). Used for brand-level profitability analysis.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System Brand Master

description: The approved annual budget amount allocated to this cost center for the current fiscal year. Used for budget control and variance analysis.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').
The business area or division to which this cost center is assigned (e.g., 'Apparel', 'Footwear', 'Accessories'). Used for segment reporting.
The sales or distribution channel to which this cost center is aligned: DTC (Direct-to-Consumer), wholesale, retail (brick-and-mortar stores), ecommerce (digital storefront), outlet (off-price).
The cumulative committed costs (purchase orders, contracts) against this cost center from the beginning of the fiscal year to the current period. Used for funds availability and budget control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Order Commitment

description: The SAP Controlling Area (CO) code to which this cost center belongs. Controlling area is the organizational unit in SAP FI/CO for cost accounting and internal reporting.

The method used to allocate costs from this cost center to other cost objects: direct (traced to specific receivers), assessment (fixed allocation), distribution (variable allocation), activity-based (driver-based), percentage (proportional split).
Extended textual description of the cost center's purpose, scope, and responsibilities. Used for documentation and user guidance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master

description: The descriptive name of the cost center (e.g., 'Design Studio NYC', 'Warehouse Operations LA', 'Marketing Digital').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master

description: The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO. Used for posting, reporting, and budget control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active: operational and accepting postings. Inactive: closed, no new postings. Blocked: temporarily suspended. Planned: future cost center not yet operational.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master

description: Classification of the cost center by functional purpose: production (manufacturing/CMT), service (support functions), administration (overhead), sales (retail/wholesale), marketing (brand/campaigns), distribution (logistics/3PL), research and development (PLM/design). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|service|administration|sales|marketing|distribution|research_development — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master

description: The cost element group or category to which the majority of costs in this cost center belong (e.g., 'Labor', 'Materials', 'Overhead', 'Depreciation'). Used for COGS buildup and cost-of-sales reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element Master

description: The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for which the actual cost and committed cost figures are reported. Used for monthly close and period-over-period analysis.
The fiscal year for which the budget and actual cost figures are reported. Used for year-over-year analysis and EOM close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Fiscal Year Variant

description: The functional classification of the cost center (e.g., 'Manufacturing', 'Logistics', 'Sales', 'Marketing', 'Administration'). Used for cost-of-sales accounting and COGS buildup.
The hierarchical node or group to which this cost center belongs in the organizational cost center hierarchy. Used for roll-up reporting and consolidation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Hierarchy

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this cost center is locked and cannot accept new cost postings. Used during period close and cost center deactivation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this cost center receives allocated overhead costs from other cost centers. True if this cost center is a receiver in allocation cycles.
Boolean flag indicating whether this cost center sends (distributes) overhead costs to other cost centers or cost objects. True if this cost center is a sender in allocation cycles.
The user ID or name of the person who last modified this cost center record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The date and time when this cost center record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
The physical location or site code where this cost center operates (e.g., warehouse code, store code, office code).
The product line or category to which this cost center is aligned (e.g., 'MENS', 'WOMENS', 'KIDS', 'ATHLETIC'). Used for product-level cost allocation.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System Merchandise Hierarchy

description: The geographic region or market to which this cost center is assigned (e.g., 'NORAM', 'EMEA', 'APAC', 'LATAM'). Used for regional profitability and cost analysis.
The name or code of the source system from which this cost center record originated (e.g., 'SAP_S4HANA_FICO', 'ANAPLAN'). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
The date from which this cost center is valid and operational. Used for time-dependent cost center master data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master Validity Period

description: The date until which this cost center is valid. Null or far-future date indicates open-ended validity. Used for cost center closure and historical reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Cost Center Master Validity Period

description: The budget variance amount (budget minus actual cost YTD). Positive indicates under-budget, negative indicates over-budget.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Budget vs Actual Report

description: The budget variance expressed as a percentage of the budget amount. Used for KPI dashboards and management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Budget vs Actual Report

description: Unique identifier for the general ledger account record in the chart of accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

description: FK to finance.company_code
Account group classification code that controls field status, number range, and account behavior in SAP FI. Groups accounts with similar characteristics and posting rules.
Extended description providing detailed explanation of the account purpose, usage guidelines, and posting rules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

description: Short descriptive name of the general ledger account for display and reporting purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

description: The externally-known unique account number used in SAP S/4HANA FI for posting transactions. Typically 4-10 digit numeric code per chart of accounts structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

description: High-level classification of the account into balance sheet (asset, liability, equity) or profit and loss (revenue, expense) categories.
Classification for balance sheet accounts distinguishing current vs non-current assets and liabilities. Not applicable for P&L accounts.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Indicates whether the account is currently blocked for all postings. Used to prevent transactions to obsolete or suspended accounts.
Classification of the account for cash flow statement preparation per IFRS/GAAP. Categorizes cash movements into operating, investing, or financing activities.
reference: IFRS IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows

description: Classification of the account by sales channel (DTC, wholesale, e-commerce, retail store) for channel-specific profitability analysis in apparel fashion business.
Four-character code identifying the chart of accounts to which this account belongs. Supports multi-COA scenarios for global enterprises.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Relevant Flag
description: Indicates whether this account is included in COGS calculation. Essential for gross margin analysis and inventory valuation in apparel manufacturing and retail.
Account number in the group chart of accounts used for financial consolidation and elimination of intercompany transactions at corporate level.
Indicates whether a cost center assignment is mandatory when posting to this account. Enforces cost allocation discipline for expense accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting

description: Classification of cost element as primary (posted from FI) or secondary (internal CO allocations). Not applicable for non-cost-element accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element Accounting

description: Indicates whether this account is relevant for Controlling (CO) module and functions as a cost element for cost center accounting, internal orders, or profitability analysis.
Timestamp when the general ledger account record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for chart of accounts governance.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for accounts denominated in a specific currency. Blank for accounts managed in company code currency.

business_glossary_term: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) Relevant Flag
description: Indicates whether postings to this account are included in EBITDA calculation. Critical for apparel fashion profitability analysis and investor reporting.
Four-character code defining which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting to this account. Controls data entry behavior in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Field Status Configuration

description: Detailed financial statement line item classification for statutory reporting. Maps account to specific line items in balance sheet, income statement, or cash flow statement per IFRS/GAAP requirements.
Classification of the account by business function (e.g., production, sales, administration, R&D) for functional cost-of-sales reporting per IFRS requirements.
US GAAP classification code for accounts used in US statutory reporting. Supports dual-reporting requirements for multinational apparel companies.
Current lifecycle status of the general ledger account indicating whether it is actively used, retired, or awaiting approval for use.

business_glossary_term: Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) Category
description: Classification for GMROI analysis in apparel merchandising. Links account to inventory investment and margin performance metrics by product category or channel.
Detailed IFRS standard and paragraph reference for account classification. Ensures compliance with international accounting standards for statutory reporting.
Code indicating whether and how interest is calculated on balances in this account. Used for customer/vendor interest calculation and treasury management.
Classification of the account as a component of landed cost calculation (freight, duty, insurance, customs). Critical for apparel import cost accounting and FOB to LDP reconciliation.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to the account master data. Supports change tracking and compliance audit requirements.
Indicates whether individual line items are stored and displayable for this account. Required for detailed transaction analysis and audit trails.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item Display

description: Indicates whether the account is managed on an open item basis, requiring explicit clearing of line items. Essential for AP, AR, and clearing accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Open Item Management

description: Defines the organizational level at which budget planning and control is performed for this account (e.g., company code, profit center, cost center).
Indicates whether direct manual or automated postings are permitted to this account. Some accounts may be restricted to system-only postings or blocked entirely.
Indicates whether a profit center assignment is mandatory when posting to this account. Used to enforce segment reporting and profitability analysis by business unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Profit Center Accounting

description: Detailed classification for income statement accounts including COGS, operating expenses, EBITDA components, and non-operating items. Used for margin analysis and profitability reporting.

Indicates whether this is a reconciliation account (subledger control account) that summarizes detailed postings from subsidiary ledgers such as Accounts Payable (AP), Accounts Receivable (AR), or asset accounting.
Three-character code controlling the allocation and sorting of line items within the account. Used for automatic payment programs and dunning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item Management

description: Tax classification code indicating whether the account is subject to input tax, output tax, non-taxable, or tax-exempt treatment. Used for VAT/GST reporting and compliance.
Four-character code defining posting and clearing tolerances for the account. Controls maximum amounts and variances permitted in transactions.
Date from which the account is valid and available for posting. Supports time-dependent chart of accounts changes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Time-Dependent Master Data

description: Date until which the account is valid. Null for accounts with indefinite validity. Used to phase out obsolete accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Time-Dependent Master Data

description: Unique identifier for the accounting ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Primary key for the ledger entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Ledger Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Ledgers belong to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.

The accounting standard or framework applied in this ledger (e.g., IFRS, US GAAP, local country GAAP, management accounting, tax accounting). Determines valuation rules, recognition policies, and reporting requirements.
reference: International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), US Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP)

description: Status of accrual and deferral postings for the current period. Accruals ensure expenses and revenues are recognized in the correct period per the applicable accounting principle (IFRS, GAAP). Pending indicates accruals not yet posted; complete indicates all accruals posted; partial indicates some accruals posted.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the primary currency for this ledger (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All financial amounts in this ledger are expressed or translated into this currency.
The chart of accounts used in this ledger, defining the structure and numbering of general ledger (GL) accounts. Supports operational, group, and country-specific charts of accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts Configuration

description: Free-text description of the issue preventing period close completion when close_checklist_status is blocked. Examples: unreconciled intercompany balances, missing accruals, pending depreciation run, outstanding approvals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Close Issue Tracking

description: Identifier or name of the close calendar template defining the structured sequence of close tasks, milestones, and deadlines for this ledger. Templates standardize close processes across periods and company codes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Close Calendar Configuration

description: Overall status of the period-end close checklist for the current period. Not started indicates close has not begun; in progress indicates active close tasks; complete indicates all steps finished and approved; blocked indicates close is halted due to issues.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Close Task Management

description: Name or identifier of the finance team member or role responsible for managing the period-end close process for this ledger. Accountable for completing close checklist steps and obtaining sign-off approvals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Close Process Governance

description: Identifier of the consolidation group or reporting entity to which this ledger belongs. Used for group-level financial consolidation and statutory reporting across multiple company codes and legal entities.
Indicates whether this ledger is used for group consolidation and statutory reporting. True if the ledger feeds consolidation systems; false for local or management ledgers.
Date and time when this ledger record was first created in SAP S/4HANA FI. Audit field for configuration change tracking.

Defines the currency perspective for this ledger. Company code currency is local operational currency; group currency is the consolidation currency; hard currency is a stable reference currency; index currency supports inflation-adjusted reporting.
The fiscal year currently active for posting and reporting in this ledger (e.g., 2024). Updated at fiscal year rollover.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Management

description: The posting period currently open for transaction entry in this ledger (1-16, where 1-12 are regular periods and 13-16 are special periods for adjustments). Controlled by period-end close process.
Status of the periodic depreciation calculation and posting run for fixed assets in this ledger. Depreciation run is a mandatory step in the End of Month (EOM) close process.
The date on which this ledger was closed or deactivated. Null for active ledgers. Used for historical ledgers that have been archived or replaced.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Ledger Lifecycle Management

description: The date from which this ledger became active and began accepting financial postings. Represents the go-live date for the ledger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Ledger Lifecycle Management

description: SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal calendar structure for this ledger (number of periods, period start/end dates, year-end date). Supports calendar year, non-calendar year, and special period configurations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant Configuration

description: Status of intercompany balance reconciliation for the current period. Reconciled indicates all intercompany transactions match between trading partners; variance identified indicates discrepancies requiring resolution before close.
The date on which the most recent period close was completed and signed off. Represents the last successful End of Month (EOM) close milestone.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Period Close Management

description: The most recent posting period that has completed the End of Month (EOM) close process and is locked for further postings. Used to track close progress and enforce period controls.

Indicates whether this is the leading ledger (primary book of record) for the organization. True for the leading ledger; false for extension or special purpose ledgers. Only one leading ledger is permitted per client.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI New General Ledger Architecture

description: Short alphanumeric code identifying the ledger in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., 0L for leading ledger, 2L for IFRS ledger). Used as the business identifier for the ledger across financial transactions and reporting.
Full descriptive name of the accounting ledger (e.g., Leading Ledger, IFRS Ledger, Management Accounting Ledger). Human-readable label for business users.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Ledger Master Data

description: Current operational status of the ledger. Active ledgers accept postings; inactive ledgers are configured but not yet in use; closed ledgers are archived and read-only; suspended ledgers are temporarily blocked.

Classification of the ledger role in the accounting architecture. Leading ledger is the primary book of record; extension ledgers support parallel accounting views (IFRS, local GAAP); special purpose ledgers support management accounting or simulation scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI New General Ledger

description: SAP user ID of the person who last modified this ledger configuration. Used for audit trail and change management.
Date and time when this ledger record was last modified in SAP S/4HANA FI. Audit field for configuration change tracking.

Indicates whether this ledger is part of a multi-GAAP parallel accounting setup, supporting simultaneous compliance with multiple accounting standards (e.g., IFRS and US GAAP). True if multi-GAAP; false if single standard.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Parallel Accounting for Multi-GAAP

description: The target date by which the next period close must be completed. Drives the close calendar and task scheduling for depreciation runs, accruals, intercompany reconciliation, and statutory reporting milestones.
Free-text field for additional comments, configuration notes, or special instructions related to this ledger. Used for documentation and knowledge transfer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Ledger Master Data

description: Describes the specific parallel accounting use case this ledger supports (e.g., IFRS 16 Leases, US GAAP Revenue Recognition, Local Tax Reporting). Free-text field for business context.

Indicates whether the current posting period is open for all users, locked to prevent further postings, or partially locked (open only for specific user groups or account ranges). Managed during End of Month (EOM) close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Period Close Controls

description: SAP posting period variant controlling the number of posting periods and special periods (e.g., 12 regular periods plus 4 special periods for year-end adjustments). Governs period open/close controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period Configuration

description: Name or identifier of the senior finance leader (e.g., CFO, Controller, Finance Director) responsible for final approval and sign-off of the period close for this ledger. Approval confirms financial statements are complete and accurate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Close Approval Workflow

description: The date on which the sign-off approver formally approved the period close and financial statements for this ledger. Marks the official completion of the close process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Close Approval Workflow

description: Status of statutory financial reporting deliverables for this ledger and period (e.g., balance sheet, income statement, cash flow statement per IFRS or local GAAP). Not started indicates reports not yet generated; submitted indicates reports filed with authorities; approved indicates reports signed off by auditors or management.
reference: International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), Local GAAP Requirements

description: The valuation approach applied in this ledger. Legal valuation follows local statutory rules; group valuation applies corporate consolidation policies; profit center and segment valuations support internal management reporting.
SAP user ID of the person who created this ledger configuration in the system. Used for audit trail and configuration governance.
Unique identifier for the profit center in SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA. Primary key for the profit center master record.

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Profit centers belong to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.

Method used for allocating shared costs and overhead to this profit center. Direct for directly traceable costs; step-down for sequential allocation; reciprocal for mutual service allocation; activity-based for ABC costing.
Fiscal period for which profit center performance is analyzed. Format YYYYMM (e.g., 202312 for December 2023). Used for period-over-period EBITDA and margin analysis.
Brand identifier for brand-level profit centers in multi-brand apparel fashion companies. Used for brand profitability and EBITDA contribution analysis.
SAP Business Area code for cross-company code reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements across multiple legal entities for a specific business segment.

Sales channel classification for channel-level profitability analysis. DTC includes owned stores and brand.com; wholesale includes department stores and specialty retailers; retail includes franchise and licensed stores; ecommerce includes third-party digital platforms; marketplace includes Amazon, Zalando, etc.
Code identifying the consolidation unit for group-level financial consolidation. Used for eliminating intercompany transactions and preparing consolidated financial statements.
SAP Controlling Area to which this profit center is assigned. Controlling area represents the organizational unit for cost accounting and profitability analysis.
Code identifying the group of cost centers that roll up into this profit center for cost allocation and overhead absorption in COGS calculation.
User ID of the person who created the profit center master record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when the profit center master record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the profit centers local currency. Used for revenue recognition, COGS accounting, and margin analysis before consolidation.
Department code for store-level or functional profit centers. Used for department-level profitability in multi-department retail stores.
Geographic region for regional profit centers. Used for regional profitability analysis and landed cost (LDP) accounting by market.
Code identifying the profit center hierarchy (standard hierarchy) to which this profit center belongs. Used for multi-level profitability reporting and roll-up analysis.
User ID of the person who last modified the profit center master record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp when the profit center master record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.

Flag indicating whether the profit center is locked for transaction posting. True when locked (no new postings allowed); False when unlocked (normal operations). Used during EOM close and financial consolidation.
Free-text notes and comments about the profit center configuration, special accounting treatments, or organizational changes. Used for audit trail and knowledge management.
Planning granularity level for financial planning and budgeting. Strategic for division-level; tactical for brand/channel-level; operational for store/product-line level.
Primary product category for product-line profit centers. Used for GMROI and margin analysis by category.
Business identifier for the profit center used in SAP S/4HANA. Externally-known alphanumeric code representing the autonomous business segment (e.g., brand, channel, product line, region).
Full descriptive name of the profit center. Human-readable label identifying the business segment (e.g., 'Mens Footwear DTC', 'Womens Apparel Wholesale', 'EMEA Retail Operations').

Current lifecycle status of the profit center. Active profit centers are operational and accumulate financial transactions; planned profit centers are configured but not yet operational; inactive profit centers are temporarily suspended; closed profit centers are permanently retired.

Classification of the profit center by organizational dimension. Indicates whether the profit center represents a brand, sales channel (DTC/wholesale/retail), product line, geographic region, individual store, or business division.
Email address of the profit center manager for financial reporting notifications and EBITDA analysis distribution.
Name of the business leader or manager accountable for the profit centers financial performance. Typically a brand director, channel head, regional VP, or product line manager.
IFRS segment code for segment reporting under IFRS 8. Represents operating segments for external financial reporting and investor disclosures.
Abbreviated name or acronym for the profit center used in reports and dashboards for space-constrained displays.
Tax jurisdiction code for statutory tax reporting and compliance. Used for VAT/GST reporting and transfer pricing documentation.
Transfer pricing method for intercompany transactions between profit centers. Used for internal revenue recognition and OECD transfer pricing compliance.
Date from which the profit center becomes effective and begins accumulating financial transactions. Used for time-dependent profit center master data.
Date until which the profit center remains effective. Nullable for open-ended profit centers. Used for profit center lifecycle management and historical analysis.

description: Unique identifier for the COGS accounting entry. Primary key for the COGS entry record.

Foreign key linking to product.colorway. Business justification: cogs_entry.colorway_code is a plain denormalized text field. Adding colorway_id FK normalizes COGS-to-colorway linkage for colorway-level margin analysis. Apparel buyers use colorway COGS data to iden

Foreign key linking to finance.landed_cost. Business justification: COGS entries in apparel fashion capture LDP cost components (ldp_cost, fob_cost, duty_cost, freight_cost) that are derived from landed cost records. Adding landed_cost_id links the COGS recognition ev

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: COGS recognition requires traceability to production lot for FIFO/LIFO costing methods and margin analysis by batch quality/origin. Essential for apparel cost accounting and inventory valuation compli

Reference to the original COGS entry ID if this entry is a reversal or adjustment, supporting audit trail and reconciliation.

Reference to the retail store where the sale occurred, if applicable to retail channel transactions.
Reference to the sales order that triggered this COGS recognition event.
Reference to the specific sales order line item for which COGS is being recognized.

Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: COGS variance analysis in apparel fashion requires tracing recognized cost-of-goods back to the originating sourcing PO to compare standard vs actual cost. cogs_entry currently stores purchase_order_n

Foreign key linking to product.style. Business justification: cogs_entry.style_code is a plain denormalized text field. Adding style_id FK normalizes COGS-to-style linkage for style-level profitability analysis. Apparel merchandising and finance teams report gro
Reference to the supplier or manufacturer who produced this product, supporting supplier profitability analysis.
Reference to the warehouse or distribution center from which the product was fulfilled.
The actual cost per unit incurred for this SKU, including all manufacturing and procurement costs.
The collection or line code for the product, supporting collection-level margin and IMU/MMU tracking.
The difference between standard cost and actual cost per unit, used for cost control and variance analysis.
The inventory costing method applied to calculate the COGS for this entry (standard, average, FIFO, LIFO, or specific identification).
The user ID or system process that created this COGS entry record.
The timestamp when this COGS entry record was first created in the system.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this COGS entry.
The accounting document number in the financial system for this COGS posting, supporting audit and reconciliation.
The customs duty and tariff cost component allocated to this unit for international shipments.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for this COGS entry, supporting EOM close and period reporting.
The fiscal year in which this COGS entry is recognized for financial reporting purposes.
The FOB cost component representing the cost of goods at the point of shipment from the supplier or manufacturer.
The freight and transportation cost component allocated to this unit for shipping from origin to destination.
The user ID or system process that last modified this COGS entry record.
The timestamp when this COGS entry record was last modified or updated.
The total landed cost including FOB, freight, duty, and all other costs to deliver the product to the final destination.
The date on which this COGS entry was posted to the general ledger, which may differ from recognition date due to period-end adjustments.
The merchandise category code for the product, enabling category-level profitability analysis.
The quantity of units sold for this SKU in this transaction, triggering the COGS recognition.
The accounting date on which the COGS was recognized, typically aligned with revenue recognition per IFRS 15 or GAAP ASC 606.
Boolean flag indicating whether this COGS entry is a reversal or adjustment of a previous entry.
The sales channel through which the product was sold, enabling channel-specific GMROI and margin analysis.
The geographic region code where the sale occurred, enabling regional profitability analysis.
The merchandising season code for the product sold, enabling seasonal profitability and GMROI analysis.
The size code for the product (e.g., XS, S, M, L, XL, or numeric sizing).
The predetermined standard cost per unit for this SKU as maintained in the cost accounting system, used for variance analysis.

business_glossary_term: Total Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Amount
description: The total COGS amount for this entry, calculated as actual cost multiplied by quantity sold.
The SAP valuation class used for inventory valuation and COGS calculation, supporting material ledger accounting.
Primary key for landed_cost

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Landed cost records represent imported goods cost breakdowns and must be tied to a legal entity (company code) for proper financial accounting, statutory reporting, and consolidation. This FK is essen

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Landed costs are allocated to cost centers in SAP S/4HANA CO for overhead cost accounting and GMROI analysis. landed_cost has cost_allocation_method indicating cost center allocation occurs, but lacks

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Landed cost records are posted to specific GL accounts in SAP S/4HANA FI (e.g., customs duty expense, freight-in, insurance). landed_cost has gl_posting_date indicating GL postings occur, but lacks a
Reference to the goods receipt document that triggered the landed cost calculation. Links to the inbound inventory receipt event in SAP S/4HANA MM.

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Landed cost records are posted to a specific accounting ledger (IFRS, GAAP, or local statutory) in SAP S/4HANA parallel accounting. landed_cost has fiscal_period and fiscal_year but no ledger_id FK. A

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Landed costs must be allocated to production lots for accurate unit cost calculation and COGS recognition. Apparel costing requires lot-level traceability for dye lots, factory batches, and origin-spe

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Landed costs are allocated to profit centers in SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA for profitability analysis by channel, product line, and region. landed_cost has no profit_center_id FK despite being critical for GM
Reference to the specific SKU for which the landed cost is being calculated. Identifies the product at the most granular inventory level.

Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Landed cost (duty, freight, customs) in apparel fashion is calculated and allocated per sourcing PO. Finance teams run landed cost reports by PO to validate LDP estimates vs actuals. landed_cost alrea

Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Landed cost calculations are factory-specific in apparel fashion — duty rates, compliance surcharges, and freight costs vary by factory location and certification status. Linking landed_cost to suppli
Reference to the supplier or vendor from whom the goods were sourced. Links to the supplier master record.
The cost of air freight transportation if goods were shipped by air. Component of landed cost for expedited shipments.
The date on which the landed cost calculation was performed. Used for financial period assignment and audit purposes.
The method used to allocate shared costs (freight, insurance) across multiple SKUs in a shipment. Common methods: weight-based, volume-based, value-based, unit count.
The date and time when this landed cost record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this landed cost record. Typically the functional currency of the importing entity.
The fee paid to customs brokers for handling import documentation and customs clearance procedures.
The customs duty paid to import the goods into the destination country. Calculated based on HS code tariff rate and customs value.
The declared value of the goods for customs purposes. Basis for calculating customs duty and import taxes.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the destination country where the goods are imported. Determines applicable tariff rates and customs regulations.
The foreign exchange rate applied to convert supplier invoice amounts to the functional currency. Used when FOB price is in a different currency.
reference: IFRS IAS 21 - The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the landed cost was recognized. Used for monthly financial close and reporting.
The fiscal year in which the landed cost was recognized for financial reporting purposes.
The FOB price of the goods as agreed with the supplier. Represents the cost of goods before international freight and insurance.
The date on which the landed cost was posted to the general ledger. Determines the financial period for COGS recognition and inventory valuation.
The date on which the goods were physically received into inventory. Determines the accounting period for cost recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt Posting Date

business_glossary_term: Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) Preference Applied
description: Indicates whether a GSP tariff preference was applied to reduce or eliminate customs duty. True if GSP benefits were utilized.

business_glossary_term: Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) Preference Rate Percent
description: The reduced tariff rate percentage applied under GSP preference. Null if no GSP preference was applied.
The Harmonized System tariff classification code for the imported goods. Determines the applicable customs duty rate and trade regulations.

The Incoterms 2020 code defining the terms of delivery and cost responsibility between buyer and seller. Common values: FOB, CIF, EXW, FCA, CPT, CIP, DAP, DPU, DDP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The cost of domestic transportation from the port of discharge to the final warehouse or distribution center.
The cost of cargo insurance covering the goods during international transit. Component of landed cost.
Business identifier for the landed cost calculation. Human-readable reference number used in financial reporting and audit trails.

The current status of the landed cost record in the financial workflow. Values: draft (initial calculation), calculated (computation complete), approved (reviewed and approved), posted (recorded to general ledger), reversed (corrected or cancelled).
The letter of credit number used to secure payment for this shipment. Links to trade finance instruments.
reference: UCP 600 - Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits

description: The date and time when this landed cost record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates and corrections.
The cost of ocean freight transportation from the port of loading to the port of discharge. Component of landed cost.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of manufacture or origin for the goods. Used for customs duty calculation and trade compliance.
The cost of port handling services including unloading, storage, and terminal fees at the port of discharge.
The seaport or airport where the goods were unloaded upon arrival. Used for customs clearance and port handling cost allocation.
reference: UN/LOCODE - Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: The seaport or airport from which the goods were shipped. Used for freight cost allocation and logistics tracking.
reference: UN/LOCODE - Code for Trade and Transport Locations

description: The quantity of goods received in this shipment. Used to calculate unit landed cost and allocate costs across units.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt Quantity

description: The customs duty rate percentage applied to the goods based on the HS code and origin country. Used to calculate the customs duty amount.

business_glossary_term: Total Landed Duty Paid (LDP) Cost Amount
description: The total landed duty paid cost per unit or shipment. Sum of FOB price, freight, insurance, customs duty, and all other charges. Authoritative cost basis for inventory valuation and COGS.
The landed cost per individual unit of the SKU. Calculated by dividing total landed cost by quantity received. Used for per-unit inventory valuation.

The unit of measure for the quantity received. Standard units include EA (each), PC (piece), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), M (meter), YD (yard), PAIR, SET, BOX, CASE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|YD|PAIR|SET|BOX|CASE — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Unique identifier for the letter of credit record. Primary key.
Self-referencing FK on letter_of_credit (amended_letter_of_credit_id)

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Letters of credit are issued by a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.

Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Letters of credit are issued through a specific bank account maintained in SAP S/4HANA FI. Currently the issuing bank is captured as denormalized STRING fields (issuing_bank_name, issuing_bank_swift_c

Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: In apparel fashion, Letters of Credit are issued specifically against sourcing POs as the primary vendor payment instrument for overseas factory orders. Finance and sourcing teams must reconcile LC ut

Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor_bank_account. Business justification: LC settlement requires identifying the exact vendor bank account designated as beneficiary. In apparel fashion, LC payment processing and bank document preparation depend on linking the LC to the spec
Foreign key reference to the supplier master data. Links the LC beneficiary to the internal supplier record for procurement and payment tracking.
The name of the bank that advises the letter of credit to the beneficiary, typically located in the beneficiary's country.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The SWIFT/BIC code of the advising bank for international communication and document handling.
The date of the most recent amendment to the letter of credit terms.
Sequential number tracking amendments made to the original letter of credit. Zero indicates the original LC with no amendments.
The full business address of the applicant party requesting the letter of credit.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The name of the party (buyer/importer) who requests the issuing bank to open the letter of credit.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The remaining credit amount available for utilization under the letter of credit.
The full business address of the beneficiary party entitled to receive payment under the letter of credit.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The name of the party (seller/exporter/supplier) in whose favor the letter of credit is issued and who is entitled to draw under it.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: Indicates which party (applicant or beneficiary) is responsible for paying the letter of credit charges and fees.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The name of the bank that adds its confirmation to the letter of credit, providing additional payment guarantee to the beneficiary.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The user ID or name of the person who created this letter of credit record in the system.
The timestamp when this letter of credit record was first created in the system. Record audit trail for data governance.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the letter of credit amount.

Detailed description of all documents required to be presented for negotiation under the letter of credit (e.g., commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading, certificate of origin, inspection certificate).
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The date when the letter of credit expires and is no longer valid for presentation of documents. Represents the effective-until date of the agreement.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: Description of the goods or merchandise covered by the letter of credit, including product details, quantities, and specifications.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery terms, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and seller. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date when the letter of credit was officially issued by the issuing bank. This is the principal business event timestamp marking when the LC becomes effective.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The user ID or name of the person who last modified this letter of credit record in the system.
The timestamp when this letter of credit record was last updated in the system. Record audit trail for data governance.
The last permissible date for shipment of goods under the terms of the letter of credit.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The maximum monetary value of the letter of credit. This is the principal quantitative fact representing the credit limit available to the beneficiary.
The externally-known documentary credit number issued by the bank. This is the business identifier used in trade finance communications and documentation.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: Current lifecycle status of the letter of credit in the trade finance workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|advised|confirmed|amended|utilized|expired|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Classification of the letter of credit based on its terms and conditions. Determines the level of commitment and transferability.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: Indicates whether partial shipments of goods are permitted under the letter of credit terms.
The payment terms specified in the letter of credit indicating when payment will be made (at sight, deferred, or upon acceptance).
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The port or place where goods are unloaded at destination under the letter of credit terms.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The port or place where goods are loaded for shipment under the letter of credit terms.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The maximum number of days after shipment date within which documents must be presented to the bank for negotiation.
Indicates whether the letter of credit automatically renews for additional shipments after each utilization.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: Any additional special terms, conditions, or instructions specific to this letter of credit that are not covered by standard fields.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The number of days after sight or shipment date when payment is due under deferred payment or usance terms.
reference: UCP 600 (Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits)

description: The permissible percentage variance (plus or minus) allowed in the LC amount and quantity as per UCP 600 Article 30.
Indicates whether the letter of credit can be transferred in whole or in part to one or more second beneficiaries.
Indicates whether transshipment (transfer of goods from one vessel to another during transit) is permitted under the letter of credit terms.
The cumulative amount that has been drawn or utilized against the letter of credit to date.
