reference: IFRS IAS 21, SAP S/4HANA Local Currency Accounting

description: Free-text field used for additional sorting and grouping of line items. Often contains purchase order numbers, project codes, or other business references for reconciliation.
Four-character code representing a business segment or division for internal management reporting. Enables profitability analysis by business unit across company codes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure, IFRS 8 - Operating Segments

description: System timestamp when this journal entry record was first created in the SAP system. Used for audit trail and process performance monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail, SOX Compliance

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the document currency (transaction currency). All line items in the document are posted in this currency.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes, IFRS IAS 21 - Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Indicator for debit (S=Soll/debit) or credit (H=Haben/credit) posting. Fundamental accounting classification for double-entry bookkeeping.
reference: Double-Entry Bookkeeping Principles, SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Logic

description: The date on the source document (invoice, receipt, etc.) that originated this journal entry. May differ from posting date due to processing delays.
Free-text description at the journal entry header level. Provides business context for the entire document (e.g., 'Month-end accrual for freight costs', 'Intercompany elimination Q1 2024').
The externally-known accounting document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA FI. Unique within company code and fiscal year. Used for audit trail and cross-reference.

Two-character code classifying the journal entry by business transaction type (e.g., SA=GL account document, DR=customer invoice, KR=vendor invoice, AB=accounting document). Controls number ranges and posting rules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Type Configuration

description: The date when the journal entry was physically entered into the SAP system by the user. Used for audit trail and process monitoring.
The exchange rate used to convert document currency to local currency. Applied at document header level for all line items.
reference: IFRS IAS 21, SAP S/4HANA Currency Translation

description: Code identifying the type of exchange rate used (e.g., M=average rate, B=bank buying rate, G=bank selling rate). Determines which rate table is used for currency conversion.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Currency Configuration, IFRS IAS 21

description: The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the entry was posted. Typically 1-12 for regular periods, 13-16 for special closing periods.
reference: IFRS IAS 1, SAP S/4HANA Fiscal Year Variant

description: The fiscal year in which the journal entry was posted. Used for period-based financial reporting and statutory compliance.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 - Presentation of Financial Statements, GAAP ASC 205

description: Classification of costs and revenues by business function (e.g., production, sales, administration, R&D). Required for cost-of-sales accounting and functional P&L statements per IFRS.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 - Presentation of Financial Statements (functional classification), SAP S/4HANA Functional Area

description: Flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction requiring elimination during financial consolidation. True for intercompany, False for external transactions.
SAP user ID of the person who last modified this journal entry. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404, SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: System timestamp when this journal entry record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit and compliance purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail, SOX Compliance

description: Free-text description at the line item level providing business context for the specific posting (e.g., 'Accrued freight for shipment #12345', 'COGS adjustment for SKU ABC123').
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company code's local currency. Used for statutory reporting and local GAAP compliance.

The date on which the journal entry was posted to the general ledger. This is the accounting date that determines the fiscal period and affects financial statement balances. Critical for period-end close (EOM) and financial reporting.
reference: IFRS IAS 1, GAAP ASC 205, SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Logic

description: Two-digit code that controls the type of posting (debit/credit) and the account type (GL, customer, vendor, asset). Determines field status and posting behavior in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Key Configuration

description: External reference number from source document (invoice number, PO number, etc.). Used for reconciliation and cross-reference to operational documents.
The date on which this journal entry was reversed, if applicable. Null if not reversed. Used for tracking accrual reversals and corrections.
Flag indicating whether this journal entry has been reversed. True if reversed, False if active. Used for period-end adjustments and error corrections.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Processing, IFRS IAS 8 - Accounting Policies

description: Organizational unit for segment reporting per IFRS 8. Represents an operating segment for which discrete financial information is available and regularly reviewed by chief operating decision maker.
reference: IFRS 8 - Operating Segments, SAP S/4HANA Segment Reporting

description: The calculated tax amount for this line item in local currency. Automatically derived from tax code and base amount.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Calculation, Local Tax Regulations

description: Two-character code identifying the tax rate and tax type (VAT, sales tax, use tax) applied to this line item. Drives automatic tax calculation and tax reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration, Local Tax Regulations

description: Identifier for the intercompany trading partner in intercompany transactions. Used for intercompany eliminations and consolidation in multi-entity organizations.
reference: IFRS 10 - Consolidated Financial Statements, SAP S/4HANA Intercompany Processing

description: The SAP transaction code (T-code) used to create this journal entry (e.g., FB01, FB50, F-02). Indicates whether entry was manual or automated from SD/MM/PP modules.
The date used to determine the exchange rate for currency translation. Typically the posting date or document date.
reference: IFRS IAS 21, SAP S/4HANA Currency Translation

description: Project Systems work breakdown structure element for project-related postings. Enables project cost tracking and capital expenditure management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Project Systems, PMI PMBOK

description: SAP user ID of the person who created this journal entry. Used for audit trail, segregation of duties monitoring, and compliance with SOX controls.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404, SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for the journal entry line detail record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Line Item

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry lines can be allocated to cost centers for cost tracking. Normalizing STRING cost_center_code to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_code STRING

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry lines post to GL accounts for financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column.
Reference to the parent journal entry document header. Links this line item to its controlling document.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Journal entry lines can be allocated to profit centers for profitability analysis. Normalizing STRING profit_center_code to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profi

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: journal_entry_line.material_number is a plain denormalized text field mapping to apparel SKUs. Adding sku_id FK normalizes product-level GL line item reporting, enabling SKU-level financial drill-down
Monetary amount of the line item converted to the corporate group reporting currency. Used for consolidated financial statements and group-level EBITDA analysis.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, SAP S/4HANA Group Currency

description: Monetary amount of the line item converted to the company code local currency. Used for statutory reporting and local GAAP compliance.
reference: IAS 21 Foreign Currency Translation, SAP S/4HANA Local Currency

description: Monetary amount of the line item in the original transaction currency. Used for multi-currency operations and foreign exchange tracking.
reference: IAS 21 Foreign Currency Translation, SAP S/4HANA Currency Management

description: Free-text assignment field for additional sorting and grouping criteria. Often used to store reference numbers, batch identifiers, or custom allocation keys.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Assignment Field

description: Baseline date for payment terms calculation. Starting point for determining due dates and cash discount periods in AP/AR processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Payment Terms, Accounts Payable/Receivable Standards

description: Business area classification for cross-company code segment reporting. Used for creating separate balance sheets and P&L statements by business division.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Area Accounting, IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: Date when this line item was cleared or settled. Used for open item management in AP/AR and reconciliation processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Clearing, Accounts Reconciliation Standards

description: Document number of the clearing transaction that settled this line item. Links open items to their corresponding payment or offset entries.
Date and time when this journal entry line item record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail, SOX Compliance, GDPR Article 5

description: Customer account number for accounts receivable postings. Links financial transactions to customer master data for AR aging and revenue recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master, Accounts Receivable Standards, IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Indicates whether this line item represents a debit or credit entry in the general ledger. Fundamental accounting classification for double-entry bookkeeping.
Functional area classification for cost-of-sales accounting. Distinguishes between production, sales, administration, and other functional cost categories per IFRS/GAAP requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Functional Area, IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the corporate group reporting currency. Used for consolidation and group-level financial analysis.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes, IFRS 10 Consolidated Reporting

description: Internal order number for overhead cost collection and allocation. Used for tracking costs of internal activities, events, or short-term projects.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Internal Orders, Cost Accounting Standards

description: Descriptive text explaining the business purpose or nature of this journal entry line. Provides context for auditors and financial analysts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item Text, Audit Trail Requirements

description: Sequential line item number within the journal entry document. Determines the ordering and position of this line in the posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item Numbering

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company code local currency. Typically the statutory reporting currency for the legal entity.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes, IFRS/GAAP Reporting Requirements

description: Payment terms code defining due date calculation and cash discount conditions. Used for cash flow forecasting and working capital management.
Manufacturing plant or distribution center code for location-specific cost allocation. Used for multi-site COGS analysis and supply chain cost tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Plant Master, Supply Chain Accounting

description: Two-digit code that controls whether the line is a debit or credit and determines account type behavior (asset, liability, revenue, expense). Standard SAP posting keys include 40 (debit GL), 50 (credit GL), 01 (debit customer), 11 (credit customer), 21 (debit vendor), 31 (credit vendor).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Key Configuration

description: Quantity of goods or materials associated with this line item. Used for inventory movements, COGS calculation, and unit cost analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Quantity Management, IAS 2 Inventories

description: First reference key field for linking to source documents or external systems. Commonly used for invoice numbers, PO numbers, or EDI transaction references.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reference Fields, EDI Standards

description: Second reference key field for additional cross-reference tracking. Supports multi-level document traceability and reconciliation.
Third reference key field for extended reference data. Used in complex allocation scenarios or multi-system integration environments.
Document number of the reversal transaction if this line item was reversed. Maintains complete audit trail for corrected entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Documents, Audit Trail Requirements

description: Flag indicating whether this line item has been reversed. Used for audit trail and error correction tracking in financial accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Processing, Audit Standards

description: Storage location within a plant for detailed inventory tracking. Enables warehouse-level cost allocation and inventory valuation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Location, Warehouse Accounting

description: Calculated tax amount for this line item in local currency. Automatically derived from tax code and base amount for indirect tax compliance.
reference: Local Tax Regulations, SAP S/4HANA Tax Calculation

description: Tax code that determines the tax type, rate, and GL accounts for automatic tax calculation and posting. Supports VAT, sales tax, and other indirect tax requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration, Local Tax Regulations (VAT/GST/Sales Tax)

description: Trading partner company code for intercompany transactions. Identifies the counterparty legal entity in cross-company postings for consolidation elimination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Intercompany Accounting, IFRS 10 Consolidation

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount. Represents the currency in which the original business transaction occurred.
Unit of measure for the quantity field. Standard codes include EA (each), KG (kilogram), M (meter), PC (piece), etc.
reference: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units, SAP S/4HANA UOM Configuration

description: Value date for cash management and bank reconciliation. Represents the effective date when funds are available or debited.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Cash Management, Banking Standards

description: Vendor account number for accounts payable postings. Links financial transactions to supplier master data for AP aging and payment processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master, Accounts Payable Standards

description: WBS element for project-related postings. Links financial transactions to specific project phases or deliverables in PLM and capital project accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Project System, PMI Work Breakdown Structure Standards
